1: # frozen_string_literal: true

3: module Spree
4:   # The customers cart until completed, then acts as permanent record of the transaction.
5:   #
6:   # `Spree::Order` is the heart of the Solidus system, as it acts as the customer's
7:   # cart as they shop. Once an order is complete, it serves as the
8:   # permanent record of their purchase. It has many responsibilities:
9:   #
10:   # * Records and validates attributes like `total` and relationships like
11:   # `Spree::LineItem` as an ActiveRecord model.
12:   #
13:   # * Implements a customizable state machine to manage the lifecycle of an order.
14:   #
15:   # * Implements business logic to provide a single interface for quesitons like
16:   # `checkout_allowed?` or `payment_required?`.
17:   #
18:   #  * Implements an interface for mutating the order with methods like
19:   # `empty!` and `fulfill!`.
20:   #
21:   class Order < Spree::Base
22:     ORDER_NUMBER_LENGTH = 9
23:     ORDER_NUMBER_LETTERS = false
24:     ORDER_NUMBER_PREFIX = "R"
25: 
26:     include ::Spree::Config.state_machines.order
27: 
28:     include Spree::Order::Payments
29:     include Metadata
30: 
31:     class InsufficientStock < StandardError
32:       attr_reader :items
33: 
34:       def initialize(message = nil, items: {})
35:         @items = items
36:         super(message)
37:       end
38:     end
39: 
40:     class CannotRebuildShipments < StandardError; end
41: 
42:     extend Spree::DisplayMoney
43: 
44:     money_methods(
45:       :outstanding_balance,
46:       :item_total,
47:       :adjustment_total,
48:       :included_tax_total,
49:       :additional_tax_total,
50:       :tax_total,
51:       :shipment_total,
52:       :total,
53:       :order_total_after_store_credit,
54:       :total_available_store_credit,
55:       :item_total_before_tax,
56:       :shipment_total_before_tax,
57:       :item_total_excluding_vat,
58:       :promo_total
59:     )
60:     alias_method :display_ship_total, :display_shipment_total
61: 
62:     checkout_flow do
63:       go_to_state :address
64:       go_to_state :delivery
65:       go_to_state :payment, if: ->(order) { order.payment_required? }
66:       go_to_state :confirm
67:     end

69:     self.allowed_ransackable_associations = %w[shipments user bill_address ship_address line_items]
70:     self.allowed_ransackable_attributes = %w[completed_at created_at email number state payment_state shipment_state total store_id]

78:     belongs_to :user, class_name: Spree::UserClassHandle.new, optional: true
79: 
80:     belongs_to :bill_address, foreign_key: :bill_address_id, class_name: "Spree::Address", optional: true
81:     alias_method :billing_address, :bill_address
82:     alias_method :billing_address=, :bill_address=
83: 
84:     belongs_to :ship_address, foreign_key: :ship_address_id, class_name: "Spree::Address", optional: true
85:     alias_method :shipping_address, :ship_address

189:     scope :created_between, ->(start_date, end_date) { where(created_at: start_date..end_date) }
190:     scope :completed_between, ->(start_date, end_date) { where(completed_at: start_date..end_date) }

255:     # Indicates whether or not the user is allowed to proceed to checkout.
256:     # Currently this is implemented as a check for whether or not there is at
257:     # least one LineItem in the Order.  Feel free to override this logic in your
258:     # own application if you require additional steps before allowing a checkout.

288:       self.ship_address = bill_address if bill_address

298:       return false unless completed? && state != "canceled"

302:     def all_inventory_units_returned?
303:       # Inventory units are transitioned to the "return" state through CustomerReturn and
304:       # ReturnItem instead of using Order#inventory_units, thus making the latter method
305:       # potentially return stale data. This situation requires to *reload* `inventory_units`
306:       # in order to pick-up the latest changes and make the check on `returned?` reliable.
307:       inventory_units.reload.all?(&:returned?)
308:     end

322:     # Associates the specified user with the order.
323:     def associate_user!(user, override_email = true)
324:       self.user = user
325:       attrs_to_set = {user_id: user.try(:id)}
326:       attrs_to_set[:email] = user.try(:email) if override_email
327:       attrs_to_set[:created_by_id] = user.try(:id) if created_by.blank?
328: 
329:       if persisted?
330:         # immediately persist the changes we just made, but don't use save since we might have an invalid address associated
331:         self.class.unscoped.where(id:).update_all(attrs_to_set)
332:       end
333: 
334:       assign_attributes(attrs_to_set)
335:     end

354:     def find_line_item_by_variant(variant, options = {})
355:       line_items.detect { |line_item|
356:         line_item.variant_id == variant.id &&
357:           line_item_options_match(line_item, options)
358:       }
359:     end

361:     # This method enables extensions to participate in the
362:     # "Are these line items equal" decision.
363:     #
364:     # When adding to cart, an extension would send something like:
365:     # params[:product_customizations]=...
366:     #
367:     # and would provide:
368:     #
369:     # def product_customizations_match
370:     def line_item_options_match(line_item, options)
371:       return true unless options
372: 
373:       Spree::Config.line_item_comparison_hooks.all? { |hook|
374:         send(hook, line_item, options)
375:       }
376:     end

383:       # If reimbursement has happened add it back to total to prevent balance_due payment state
384:       # See: https://github.com/spree/spree/issues/6229

386:       if state == "canceled"

585:     def add_store_credit_payments
586:       return if user.nil?
587:       return if payments.store_credits.checkout.empty? && user.available_store_credit_total(currency:).zero?
588: 
589:       payments.store_credits.checkout.each(&:invalidate!)
590: 
591:       # this can happen when multiple payments are present, auto_capture is
592:       # turned off, and one of the payments fails when the user tries to
593:       # complete the order, which sends the order back to the 'payment' state.
594:       authorized_total = payments.pending.sum(:amount)
595: 
596:       remaining_total = outstanding_balance - authorized_total
597: 
598:       matching_store_credits = user.store_credits.where(currency:)
599: 
600:       if matching_store_credits.any?
601:         payment_method = Spree::PaymentMethod::StoreCredit.first
602:         sorter = Spree::Config.store_credit_prioritizer_class.new(matching_store_credits, self)
603: 
604:         sorter.call.each do |credit|
605:           break if remaining_total.zero?
606:           next if credit.amount_remaining.zero?
607: 
608:           amount_to_take = [credit.amount_remaining, remaining_total].min
609:           payments.create!(source: credit,
610:             payment_method:,
611:             amount: amount_to_take,
612:             state: "checkout",
613:             response_code: credit.generate_authorization_code)
614:           remaining_total -= amount_to_take
615:         end
616:       end
617: 
618:       other_payments = payments.checkout.not_store_credits
619:       if remaining_total.zero?
620:         other_payments.each(&:invalidate!)
621:       elsif other_payments.size == 1
622:         other_payments.first.update!(amount: remaining_total)
623:       end
624: 
625:       payments.reset
626: 
627:       if payments.where(state: %w[checkout pending completed]).sum(:amount) != total
628:         errors.add(:base, I18n.t("spree.store_credit.errors.unable_to_fund")) && (return false)
629:       end
630:     end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   class Order < Spree::Base
5:     module Payments
6:       # processes any pending payments and must return a boolean as it's
7:       # return value is used by the checkout state_machine to determine
8:       # success or failure of the 'complete' event for the order
9:       #
10:       # Returns:
11:       #
12:       # - true if all pending payments processed successfully
13:       #
14:       # - true if a payment failed, ie. raised a GatewayError
15:       #   which gets rescued and converted to TRUE when
16:       #   :allow_checkout_gateway_error is set to true
17:       #
18:       # - false if a payment failed, ie. raised a GatewayError
19:       #   which gets rescued and converted to FALSE when
20:       #   :allow_checkout_on_gateway_error is set to false
21:       #
22:       def process_payments!
23:         process_payments_with(:process!)
24:       end
25: 
26:       def authorize_payments!
27:         process_payments_with(:authorize!)
28:       end
29: 
30:       def capture_payments!
31:         process_payments_with(:purchase!)
32:       end
33: 
34:       def unprocessed_payments
35:         payments.select(&:checkout?)
36:       end
37: 
38:       private
39: 
40:       def process_payments_with(method_name)
41:         # Don't run if there is nothing to pay.
42:         return true if payment_total >= total
43: 
44:         unprocessed_payments.each do |payment|
45:           break if payment_total >= total
46: 
47:           payment.public_send(method_name)
48:         end
49:       rescue Core::GatewayError => error
50:         result = !!Spree::Config[:allow_checkout_on_gateway_error]
51:         errors.add(:base, error.message) && (return result)
52:       end
53:     end
54:   end
55: end

3: module Spree
4:   module Metadata
5:     extend ActiveSupport::Concern
6: 
7:     included do
8:       attribute :customer_metadata, :json, default: {}
9:       attribute :admin_metadata, :json, default: {}
10: 
11:       validate :validate_metadata_limits, if: :validate_metadata_enabled?
12:     end

17:       end
18:     end
19:

21: 
22:     def validate_metadata_enabled?
23:       Spree::Config.meta_data_validation_enabled
24:     end
25: 
26:     def validate_metadata_limits
27:       self.class.meta_data_columns.each { |column| validate_metadata_column(column) }
28:     end
29: 
30:     def validate_metadata_column(column)
31:       config = Spree::Config
32:       metadata = send(column)
33: 
34:       return if metadata.nil?
35: 
36:       # Check for maximum number of keys
37:       validate_metadata_keys_count(metadata, column, config.meta_data_max_keys)
38: 
39:       # Check for maximum key and value size
40:       metadata.each do |key, value|
41:         validate_metadata_key(key, column, config.meta_data_max_key_length)
42:         validate_metadata_value(key, value, column, config.meta_data_max_value_length)
43:       end
44:     end
45: 
46:     def validate_metadata_keys_count(metadata, column, max_keys)
47:       return unless metadata.keys.count > max_keys
48: 
49:       errors.add(column, "must not have more than #{max_keys} keys")
50:     end
51: 
52:     def validate_metadata_key(key, column, max_key_length)
53:       return unless key.to_s.length > max_key_length
54: 
55:       errors.add(column, "key '#{key}' exceeds #{max_key_length} characters")
56:     end
57: 
58:     def validate_metadata_value(key, value, column, max_value_length)
59:       return unless value.to_s.length > max_value_length
60: 
61:       errors.add(column, "value for key '#{key}' exceeds #{max_value_length} characters")
62:     end
63:   end
64: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   class OrderUpdater
5:     attr_reader :order
6:     delegate :payments, :line_items, :adjustments, :all_adjustments, :shipments, :quantity, to: :order
7: 
8:     def initialize(order)
9:       @order = order
10:     end
11: 
12:     # This is a multi-purpose method for processing logic related to changes in the Order.
13:     # It is meant to be called from various observers so that the Order is aware of changes
14:     # that affect totals and other values stored in the Order.
15:     #
16:     # This method should never do anything to the Order that results in a save call on the
17:     # object with callbacks (otherwise you will end up in an infinite recursion as the
18:     # associations try to save and then in turn try to call +update!+ again.)
19:     def recalculate
20:       order.transaction do
21:         recalculate_line_item_prices
22:         recalculate_item_count
23:         update_shipment_amounts
24:         update_totals
25:         if order.completed?
26:           recalculate_payment_state
27:           update_shipments
28:           recalculate_shipment_state
29:         end
30:         persist_totals
31:       end
32:       Spree::Bus.publish(:order_recalculated, order:)
33:     end
34:     alias_method :update, :recalculate
35:     deprecate update: :recalculate, deprecator: Spree.deprecator
36: 
37:     # Updates the +shipment_state+ attribute according to the following logic:
38:     #
39:     # shipped   when all Shipments are in the "shipped" state
40:     # partial   when at least one Shipment has a state of "shipped" and there is another Shipment with a state other than "shipped"
41:     #           or there are InventoryUnits associated with the order that have a state of "sold" but are not associated with a Shipment.
42:     # ready     when all Shipments are in the "ready" state
43:     # backorder when there is backordered inventory associated with an order
44:     # pending   when all Shipments are in the "pending" state
45:     #
46:     # The +shipment_state+ value helps with reporting, etc. since it provides a quick and easy way to locate Orders needing attention.
47:     def recalculate_shipment_state
48:       log_state_change("shipment") do
49:         order.shipment_state = determine_shipment_state
50:       end
51: 
52:       order.shipment_state
53:     end
54:     alias_method :update_shipment_state, :recalculate_shipment_state
55:     deprecate update_shipment_state: :recalculate_shipment_state, deprecator: Spree.deprecator
56: 
57:     # Recalculates the +payment_state+ attribute according to the following logic:
58:     #
59:     # paid          when +payment_total+ is equal to +total+
60:     # balance_due   when +payment_total+ is less than +total+
61:     # credit_owed   when +payment_total+ is greater than +total+
62:     # failed        when most recent payment is in the failed state
63:     # void          when the order has been canceled and the payment total is 0
64:     #
65:     # The +payment_state+ value helps with reporting, etc. since it provides a quick and easy way to locate Orders needing attention.
66:     def recalculate_payment_state
67:       log_state_change("payment") do
68:         order.payment_state = determine_payment_state
69:       end
70: 
71:       order.payment_state
72:     end
73:     alias_method :update_payment_state, :recalculate_payment_state
74:     deprecate update_payment_state: :recalculate_payment_state, deprecator: Spree.deprecator
75: 
76:     private
77: 
78:     def determine_payment_state
79:       if payments.present? && payments.valid.empty? && order.outstanding_balance != 0
80:         "failed"
81:       elsif order.state == "canceled" && order.payment_total.zero?
82:         "void"
83:       elsif order.outstanding_balance > 0
84:         "balance_due"
85:       elsif order.outstanding_balance < 0
86:         "credit_owed"
87:       else
88:         # outstanding_balance == 0
89:         "paid"
90:       end
91:     end
92: 
93:     def determine_shipment_state
94:       if order.backordered?
95:         "backorder"
96:       else
97:         # get all the shipment states for this order
98:         shipment_states = shipments.states
99:         if shipment_states.size > 1
100:           # multiple shiment states means it's most likely partially shipped
101:           "partial"
102:         else
103:           # will return nil if no shipments are found
104:           shipment_states.first
105:         end
106:       end
107:     end
108: 
109:     # This will update and select the best promotion adjustment, update tax
110:     # adjustments, update cancellation adjustments, and then update the total
111:     # fields (promo_total, included_tax_total, additional_tax_total, and
112:     # adjustment_total) on the item.
113:     # @return [void]
114:     def update_adjustments
115:       # Promotion adjustments must be applied first, then tax adjustments.
116:       # This fits the criteria for VAT tax as outlined here:
117:       # http://www.hmrc.gov.uk/vat/managing/charging/discounts-etc.htm#1
118:       # It also fits the criteria for sales tax as outlined here:

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   class OrderInventory
5:     attr_accessor :order, :line_item, :variant
6: 
7:     def initialize(order, line_item)
8:       @order = order
9:       @line_item = line_item
10:       @variant = line_item.variant
11:     end
12: 
13:     # Only verify inventory for completed orders (as orders in frontend checkout
14:     # have inventory assigned via +order.create_proposed_shipment+) or when
15:     # shipment is explicitly passed
16:     #
17:     # In case shipment is passed the stock location should only unstock or
18:     # restock items if the order is completed. That is so because stock items
19:     # are always unstocked when the order is completed through +shipment.finalize+
20:     def verify(shipment = nil)
21:       if order.completed? || shipment.present?
22: 
23:         existing_quantity = inventory_units.count
24:         desired_quantity = line_item.quantity - existing_quantity
25:         if desired_quantity > 0
26:           shipment ||= determine_target_shipment(desired_quantity)
27:           if shipment
28:             add_to_shipment(shipment, desired_quantity)
29:           else
30:             order.create_shipments_for_line_item(line_item).each do |new_shipment|
31:               new_shipment.finalize!
32:             end
33:           end
34:         elsif desired_quantity < 0
35:           remove(-desired_quantity, shipment)
36:         end
37:       end
38:     end
39: 
40:     def inventory_units
41:       line_item.inventory_units
42:     end
43: 
44:     private
45: 
46:     def remove(quantity, shipment = nil)
47:       if shipment.present?
48:         remove_from_shipment(shipment, quantity)
49:       else
50:         remove_from_any_shipment(quantity)
51:       end
52:     end
53: 
54:     # Returns either one of the shipment:
55:     #
56:     # first unshipped that already includes this variant
57:     # first unshipped that's leaving from a stock_location that stocks this variant, with availability check
58:     # first unshipped that's leaving from a stock_location that stocks this variant, without availability check
59:     def determine_target_shipment(quantity)
60:       potential_shipments = order.shipments.order(:created_at, :id).select(&:ready_or_pending?)
61: 
62:       potential_shipments.detect do |shipment|
63:         shipment.include?(variant)
64:       end || begin
65:         potential_shipments.detect do |shipment|
66:           Spree::Config.stock.quantifier_class.new(variant, shipment.stock_location).can_supply?(quantity)
67:         end
68:       end || potential_shipments.detect do |shipment|
69:         variant.stock_location_ids.include?(shipment.stock_location_id)
70:       end
71:     end
72: 
73:     def add_to_shipment(shipment, quantity)
74:       pending_units = []
75:       if variant.should_track_inventory?
76:         on_hand, back_order = shipment.stock_location.fill_status(variant, quantity)
77: 
78:         on_hand.times { pending_units << shipment.set_up_inventory("on_hand", variant, order, line_item) }
79:         back_order.times { pending_units << shipment.set_up_inventory("backordered", variant, order, line_item) }
80:       else
81:         quantity.times { pending_units << shipment.set_up_inventory("on_hand", variant, order, line_item) }
82:       end
83: 
84:       # adding to this shipment, and removing from stock_location
85:       if order.completed?
86:         Spree::Stock::InventoryUnitsFinalizer.new(pending_units).run!
87:       end
88: 
89:       quantity
90:     end
91: 
92:     def remove_from_any_shipment(quantity)
93:       order.shipments.each do |shipment|
94:         break if quantity == 0
95:         quantity -= remove_from_shipment(shipment, quantity)
96:       end
97:     end
98: 
99:     def remove_from_shipment(shipment, quantity)
100:       return 0 if quantity == 0 || shipment.shipped?
101: 
102:       shipment_units = shipment.inventory_units_for_item(line_item, variant).reject do |variant_unit|
103:         # TODO: exclude all 'shipped' states
104:         variant_unit.state == "shipped"
105:       end.sort_by(&:state)
106: 
107:       removed_quantity = 0
108: 
109:       shipment_units.each do |inventory_unit|
110:         break if removed_quantity == quantity
111:         inventory_unit.destroy
112:         removed_quantity += 1
113:       end
114: 
115:       if shipment.inventory_units.count.zero?
116:         order.shipments.destroy(shipment)
117:       end
118: 
119:       # removing this from shipment, and adding to stock_location
120:       if order.completed?
121:         shipment.stock_location.restock variant, removed_quantity, shipment
122:       end
123: 
124:       removed_quantity
125:     end
126:   end
127: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: # A service layer that handles generating Carton objects when inventory units
4: # are actually shipped.  It also takes care of things like updating order and
5: # shipment states and delivering shipment emails as needed.
6: class Spree::OrderShipping
7:   def initialize(order)
8:     @order = order
9:   end
10: 
11:   # A shortcut method that ships *all* inventory units in a shipment in a single
12:   # carton.  See also {#ship}.
13:   #
14:   # @param shipment The shipment to create a carton from.
15:   # @param external_number An optional external number. e.g. from a shipping company or 3PL.
16:   # @param tracking_number An optional tracking number.
17:   # @return The carton created.
18:   def ship_shipment(shipment, external_number: nil, tracking_number: nil, suppress_mailer: false)
19:     ship(
20:       inventory_units: shipment.inventory_units.shippable,
21:       stock_location: shipment.stock_location,
22:       address: shipment.order.ship_address,
23:       shipping_method: shipment.shipping_method,
24:       shipped_at: Time.current,
25:       external_number:,
26:       # TODO: Remove the `|| shipment.tracking` once Shipment#ship! is called by
27:       # OrderShipping#ship rather than vice versa
28:       tracking_number: tracking_number || shipment.tracking,
29:       suppress_mailer:
30:     )
31:   end
32: 
33:   # Generate a carton from the supplied inventory units and marks those units
34:   # as shipped.  Also sends shipment emails if appropriate and updates
35:   # shipment_states for associated orders.
36:   #
37:   # @param inventory_units The units to put in a carton together.
38:   # @param stock_location The location the carton shipped from.
39:   # @param address The address the carton was shipped to.
40:   # @param shipping_method Shipping method used for the carton.
41:   # @param shipped_at The time at which the shipment was shipped.
42:   # @param external_number An optional external number. e.g. from a shipping company or 3PL.
43:   # @param tracking_number An option tracking number.
44:   # @return The carton created.
45:   def ship(inventory_units:, stock_location:, address:, shipping_method:,
46:     shipped_at: Time.current, external_number: nil, tracking_number: nil, suppress_mailer: false)
47:     carton = nil
48: 
49:     Spree::InventoryUnit.transaction do
50:       inventory_units.each(&:ship!)
51: 
52:       carton = Spree::Carton.create!(
53:         stock_location:,
54:         address:,
55:         shipping_method:,
56:         inventory_units:,
57:         shipped_at:,
58:         external_number:,
59:         suppress_email: suppress_mailer,
60:         tracking: tracking_number
61:       )
62:     end
63: 
64:     inventory_units.map(&:shipment).uniq.each do |shipment|
65:       if shipment.inventory_units.reload.all? { |iu| iu.shipped? || iu.canceled? }
66:         shipment.update!(state: "shipped", shipped_at: Time.current, tracking: tracking_number)
67:       else
68:         shipment.update!(tracking: tracking_number)
69:       end
70:     end
71: 
72:     @order.shipments.reload
73:     @order.recalculate
74: 
75:     Spree::Bus.publish(:carton_shipped, carton:)
76:     carton
77:   end
78: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: # This class represents all of the actions one can take to modify an Order after it is complete
4: class Spree::OrderCancellations
5:   extend ActiveModel::Translation
6: 
7:   # If you need to message a third party service when an item is canceled then
8:   # set short_ship_tax_notifier to an object that responds to:
9:   #     #call(unit_cancels)
10:   class_attribute :short_ship_tax_notifier
11: 
12:   class << self
13:     def send_cancellation_mailer=(value)
14:       @send_cancellation_mailer = value
15: 
16:       unless value
17:         Spree.deprecator.warn "Using the `:send_cancellation_mailer` class " \
18:           "attribute is deprecated in favor of including or omitting the " \
19:           "`Spree::OrderInventoryCancellationMailerSubscriber` from " \
20:           "`Spree::Config.environment.subscribers` in an initializer."
21:       end
22:     end
23: 
24:     def send_cancellation_mailer
25:       @send_cancellation_mailer || @send_cancellation_mailer.nil?
26:     end
27:   end
28: 
29:   def initialize(order)
30:     @order = order
31:   end
32: 
33:   # Marks inventory units short shipped. Adjusts the order based on the value of the inventory.
34:   # Sends an email to the customer about what inventory has been short shipped.
35:   #
36:   # @api public
37:   #
38:   # @param [Array<InventoryUnit>] inventory_units the inventory units to be short shipped
39:   # @param [Spree.user_class] created_by the system or person that is short shipping the inventory unit
40:   #
41:   # @return [Array<UnitCancel>] the units that have been canceled due to short shipping
42:   def short_ship(inventory_units, created_by: nil)
43:     if inventory_units.map(&:order_id).uniq != [@order.id]
44:       raise ArgumentError, "Not all inventory units belong to this order"
45:     end
46: 
47:     unit_cancels = []
48: 
49:     Spree::OrderMutex.with_lock!(@order) do
50:       Spree::InventoryUnit.transaction do
51:         inventory_units.each do |iu|
52:           unit_cancels << short_ship_unit(iu, created_by:)
53:         end
54:       end
55: 
56:       @order.recalculate
57: 
58:       short_ship_tax_notifier&.call(unit_cancels)
59:     end
60: 
61:     Spree::Bus.publish(:order_short_shipped, order: @order, inventory_units:)
62:     unit_cancels
63:   end
64: 
65:   # Marks inventory unit canceled. Optionally allows specifying the reason why and who is performing the action.
66:   #
67:   # @api public
68:   #
69:   # @param [InventoryUnit] inventory_unit the inventory unit to be canceled
70:   # @param [String] reason the reason that you are canceling the inventory unit
71:   # @param [Spree.user_class] created_by the system or person that is canceling the inventory unit
72:   #
73:   # @return [UnitCancel] the unit that has been canceled
74:   def cancel_unit(inventory_unit, reason: Spree::UnitCancel::DEFAULT_REASON, created_by: nil)
75:     unit_cancel = nil
76: 
77:     Spree::OrderMutex.with_lock!(@order) do
78:       unit_cancel = Spree::UnitCancel.create!(
79:         inventory_unit:,
80:         reason:,
81:         created_by:
82:       )
83: 
84:       inventory_unit.cancel!
85:     end
86: 
87:     unit_cancel
88:   end
89: 
90:   # Reimburses inventory units due to cancellation.
91:   #
92:   # @api public
93:   # @param [Array<InventoryUnit>] inventory_units the inventory units to be reimbursed
94:   # @param [Spree.user_class] created_by the user that is performing this action
95:   # @return [Reimbursement] the reimbursement for inventory being canceled
96:   def reimburse_units(inventory_units, created_by:)
97:     reimbursement = nil
98: 
99:     Spree::OrderMutex.with_lock!(@order) do
100:       return_items = inventory_units.map(&:current_or_new_return_item)
101:       reimbursement = Spree::Reimbursement.new(order: @order, return_items:)
102:       reimbursement.return_all(created_by:)
103:     end
104: 
105:     reimbursement
106:   end
107: 
108:   private
109: 
110:   def short_ship_unit(inventory_unit, created_by: nil)
111:     unit_cancel = Spree::UnitCancel.create!(
112:       inventory_unit:,
113:       reason: Spree::UnitCancel::SHORT_SHIP,
114:       created_by:
115:     )
116:     unit_cancel.adjust!
117:     inventory_unit.cancel!
118: 
119:     unit_cancel
120:   end
121: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   # Spree::OrderMerger is responsible for taking two orders and merging them
5:   # together by adding the line items from additional orders to the order
6:   # that the OrderMerger is initialized with.
7:   #
8:   # Orders that are merged should be incomplete orders which should belong to
9:   # the same user. They should also be in the same currency.
10:   class OrderMerger
11:     # @!attribute order
12:     #   @api public
13:     #   @return [Spree::Order] The order which items wll be merged into.
14:     attr_accessor :order
15: 
16:     # Create the OrderMerger
17:     #
18:     # @api public
19:     # @param [Spree::Order] order The order which line items will be merged
20:     # into.
21:     def initialize(order)
22:       @order = order
23:     end
24: 
25:     # Merge a second order in to the order the OrderMerger was initialized with
26:     #
27:     # The line items from `other_order` will be merged in to the `order` for
28:     # this OrderMerger object. If the line items are for the same variant, it
29:     # will add the quantity of the incoming line item to the existing line item.
30:     # Otherwise, it will assign the line item to the new order.
31:     #
32:     # After the orders have been merged the `other_order` will be destroyed.
33:     #
34:     # @example
35:     #   initial_order = Spree::Order.find(1)
36:     #   order_to_merge = Spree::Order.find(2)
37:     #   merger = Spree::OrderMerger.new(initial_order)
38:     #   merger.merge!(order_to_merge)
39:     #   # order_to_merge is destroyed, initial order now contains the line items
40:     #   # of order_to_merge
41:     #
42:     # @api public
43:     # @param [Spree::Order] other_order An order which will be merged in to the
44:     # order the OrderMerger was initialized with.
45:     # @param [Spree::User] user Associate the order the user specified. If not
46:     # specified, the order user association will not be changed.
47:     # @return [void]
48:     def merge!(other_order, user = nil)
49:       if other_order.currency == order.currency
50:         other_order.line_items.each do |other_order_line_item|
51:           current_line_item = find_matching_line_item(other_order_line_item)
52:           handle_merge(current_line_item, other_order_line_item)
53:         end
54:       end
55: 
56:       set_user(user)
57:       if order.valid?
58:         persist_merge
59: 
60:         # So that the destroy doesn't take out line items which may have been re-assigned
61:         other_order.line_items.reload
62:         other_order.destroy
63:       end
64:     end
65: 
66:     private
67: 
68:     # Retreive a matching line item from the existing order
69:     #
70:     # It will compare line items based on variants, and all line item
71:     # comparison hooks on the order.
72:     #
73:     # @api private
74:     # @param [Spree::LineItem] other_order_line_item The line item from
75:     # `other_order` we are attempting to merge in.
76:     # @return [Spree::LineItem] A matching line item from the order. nil if none
77:     # exist.
78:     def find_matching_line_item(other_order_line_item)
79:       order.line_items.detect do |my_li|
80:         my_li.variant == other_order_line_item.variant &&
81:           Spree::Config.line_item_comparison_hooks.all? do |hook|
82:             order.send(hook, my_li, other_order_line_item.serializable_hash)
83:           end
84:       end
85:     end
86: 
87:     # Associate the user with the order
88:     #
89:     # @api private
90:     # @param [Spree::User] user The user to associate with the order. If nil
91:     # the order user association will remain the same. If the order is already
92:     # associated with a user, it will not be changed.
93:     # @return [void]
94:     def set_user(user)
95:       order.associate_user!(user) if !order.user && user
96:     end
97: 
98:     # Merge the `other_order_line_item` into `current_line_item`
99:     #
100:     # If `current_line_item` is nil, the `other_order_line_item` will be
101:     # re-assigned to the `order`.
102:     #
103:     # If the merged line item can not be saved, an error will be added to
104:     # `order`.
105:     #
106:     # @api private
107:     # @param [Spree::LineItem] current_line_item The line item to be merged
108:     # into. If nil, the `other_order_line_item` will be re-assigned.
109:     # @param [Spree::LineItem] other_order_line_item The line item to merge in.
110:     # @return [void]
111:     def handle_merge(current_line_item, other_order_line_item)
112:       if current_line_item
113:         current_line_item.quantity += other_order_line_item.quantity
114:         handle_error(current_line_item) unless current_line_item.save
115:       else
116:         new_line_item = order.line_items.build(other_order_line_item.attributes.except("id"))
117:         handle_error(new_line_item) unless new_line_item.save
118:       end
119:     end
120: 
121:     # Handle an error from saving the `line_item`
122:     #
123:     # This adds errors from the line item to the `errors[:base]` of the order.
124:     #
125:     # @api private
126:     # @param [Spree::LineItem] line_item The line item which could not be saved
127:     # @return [void]
128:     def handle_error(line_item)
129:       order.errors.add(:base, line_item.errors.full_messages)
130:     end
131: 
132:     # Save the order totals after merge
133:     #
134:     # It triggers the order recalculator to ensure that item counts and totals are
135:     # up to date.
136:     #
137:     # @api private
138:     # @return [void]
139:     def persist_merge
140:       order.recalculate
141:     end
142:   end
143: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   # Relatively simple class used to apply a {Spree::Tax::OrderTax} to a
5:   # {Spree::Order}.
6:   #
7:   # This class will create or update adjustments on the taxed items and remove
8:   # any now inapplicable tax adjustments from the order.
9:   class OrderTaxation
10:     # Create a new order taxation.
11:     #
12:     # @param [Spree::Order] order the order to apply taxes to
13:     # @return [Spree::OrderTaxation] a {Spree::OrderTaxation} object
14:     def initialize(order)
15:       @order = order
16:     end
17: 
18:     # Apply taxes to the order.
19:     #
20:     # This method will create or update adjustments on the order and all line
21:     # items and shipments in the order to reflect the appropriate taxes passed
22:     # in. It will also remove any now inapplicable tax adjustments.
23:     #
24:     # @param [Spree::Tax::OrderTax] taxes the taxes to apply to the order
25:     # @return [void]
26:     def apply(taxes)
27:       update_adjustments(@order, taxes.order_taxes) if taxes.order_taxes
28: 
29:       @order.line_items.each do |item|
30:         taxed_items = taxes.line_item_taxes.select { |element| element.item_id == item.id }
31:         item.tax_category_id = item.variant_tax_category_id
32:         update_adjustments(item, taxed_items)
33:       end
34: 
35:       @order.shipments.each do |item|
36:         taxed_items = taxes.shipment_taxes.select { |element| element.item_id == item.id }
37:         update_adjustments(item, taxed_items)
38:       end
39:     end
40: 
41:     private
42: 
43:     # Walk through the taxes for an item and update adjustments for it. Once
44:     # all of the taxes have been added as adjustments, remove any old tax
45:     # adjustments that weren't touched.
46:     #
47:     # @private
48:     # @param [#adjustments] item a {Spree::LineItem} or {Spree::Shipment}
49:     # @param [Array<Spree::Tax::ItemTax>] taxed_items a list of calculated taxes for an item
50:     # @return [void]
51:     def update_adjustments(item, taxed_items)
52:       tax_adjustments = item.adjustments.select(&:tax?)
53: 
54:       active_adjustments = taxed_items.map do |tax_item|
55:         update_adjustment(item, tax_item)
56:       end
57: 
58:       # Remove any tax adjustments tied to rates which no longer match.
59:       unmatched_adjustments = tax_adjustments - active_adjustments
60:       unmatched_adjustments.each(&:mark_for_destruction)
61:     end
62: 
63:     # Update or create a new tax adjustment on an item.
64:     #
65:     # @private
66:     # @param [#adjustments] item a {Spree::LineItem} or {Spree::Shipment}
67:     # @param [Spree::Tax::ItemTax] tax_item calculated taxes for an item
68:     # @return [Spree::Adjustment] the created or updated tax adjustment
69:     def update_adjustment(item, tax_item)
70:       tax_adjustment = item.adjustments.detect do |adjustment|
71:         adjustment.source == tax_item.tax_rate
72:       end
73: 
74:       tax_adjustment ||= item.adjustments.new(
75:         source: tax_item.tax_rate,
76:         order_id: item.is_a?(Spree::Order) ? item.id : item.order_id,
77:         label: tax_item.label,
78:         included: tax_item.included_in_price
79:       )
80: 
81:       tax_adjustment.amount = tax_item.amount
82:       tax_adjustment
83:     end
84:   end
85: end

18:     def call
19:       order.validate_payments_attributes(@payments_attributes)
20: 
21:       assign_order_attributes
22:       assign_payments_attributes
23: 
24:       order.save
25:     end
26:

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   module Core
5:     class StateMachines
6:       module Order
7:         module ClassMethods
8:           attr_accessor :previous_states
9:           attr_writer :next_event_transitions, :checkout_steps, :removed_transitions
10: 
11:           def checkout_flow(&block)
12:             if block_given?
13:               @checkout_flow = block
14:               define_state_machine!
15:             else
16:               @checkout_flow
17:             end
18:           end
19: 
20:           def define_state_machine!
21:             self.checkout_steps = {}
22:             self.next_event_transitions = []
23:             self.previous_states = [:cart]
24:             self.removed_transitions = []
25: 
26:             # Build the checkout flow using the checkout_flow defined either
27:             # within the Order class, or a decorator for that class.
28:             #
29:             # This method may be called multiple times depending on if the
30:             # checkout_flow is re-defined in a decorator or not.
31:             instance_eval(&checkout_flow)
32: 
33:             klass = self
34: 
35:             # To avoid multiple occurrences of the same transition being defined
36:             # On first definition, state_machines will not be defined
37:             state_machines.clear if respond_to?(:state_machines)
38:             state_machine :state, initial: :cart, use_transactions: false do
39:               klass.next_event_transitions.each { |state| transition(state.merge(on: :next)) }
40: 
41:               event :cancel do
42:                 transition to: :canceled, if: :allow_cancel?, from: :complete
43:               end
44: 
45:               event :return do
46:                 transition to: :returned, from: [:complete, :awaiting_return], if: :all_inventory_units_returned?
47:               end
48: 
49:               event :resume do
50:                 transition to: :resumed, from: :canceled, if: :canceled?
51:               end
52: 
53:               event :authorize_return do
54:                 transition to: :awaiting_return, from: :complete
55:               end
56: 
57:               event :complete do
58:                 transition to: :complete, from: klass.checkout_steps.keys.last
59:               end
60: 
61:               if states[:payment]
62:                 event :payment_failed do
63:                   transition to: :payment, from: :confirm
64:                 end
65: 
66:                 after_transition to: :complete, do: :add_payment_sources_to_wallet
67:                 before_transition to: :payment, do: :add_default_payment_from_wallet
68:                 before_transition to: :payment, do: :ensure_billing_address
69: 
70:                 before_transition to: :confirm, do: :add_store_credit_payments
71: 
72:                 # see also process_payments_before_complete below which needs to
73:                 # be added in the correct sequence.
74:               end
75: 
76:               before_transition from: :cart, do: :ensure_line_items_present
77: 
78:               if states[:address]
79:                 before_transition to: :address, do: :assign_default_user_addresses
80:                 before_transition from: :address, do: :persist_user_address!
81:               end
82: 
83:               if states[:delivery]
84:                 before_transition to: :delivery, do: :ensure_shipping_address
85:                 before_transition to: :delivery, do: :create_proposed_shipments
86:                 before_transition to: :delivery, do: :ensure_available_shipping_rates
87:               end
88: 
89:               before_transition to: :resumed, do: :ensure_line_item_variants_are_not_deleted
90:               before_transition to: :resumed, do: :validate_line_item_availability
91: 
92:               # Sequence of before_transition to: :complete
93:               # calls matter so that we do not process payments
94:               # until validations have passed
95:               before_transition to: :complete, do: :validate_line_item_availability
96:               before_transition to: :complete, do: :ensure_promotions_eligible
97:               before_transition to: :complete, do: :ensure_line_item_variants_are_not_deleted
98:               before_transition to: :complete, do: :ensure_inventory_units
99:               if states[:payment]
100:                 before_transition to: :complete, do: :process_payments_before_complete
101:               end
102: 
103:               after_transition to: :complete, do: :finalize
104:               after_transition to: :resumed, do: :after_resume
105:               after_transition to: :canceled, do: :after_cancel
106: 
107:               after_transition from: any - :cart, to: any - [:confirm, :complete] do |order|
108:                 order.recalculate
109:               end
110: 
111:               after_transition do |order, transition|
112:                 order.logger.debug "Order #{order.number} transitioned from #{transition.from} to #{transition.to} via #{transition.event}"
113:               end
114: 
115:               after_failure do |order, transition|
116:                 order.logger.debug "Order #{order.number} halted transition on event #{transition.event} state #{transition.from}: #{order.errors.full_messages.join}"
117:               end
118:             end
119:           end
120: 
121:           def go_to_state(name, options = {})
122:             checkout_steps[name] = options
123:             previous_states.each do |state|
124:               add_transition({from: state, to: name}.merge(options))
125:             end
126:             if options[:if]
127:               previous_states << name
128:             else
129:               self.previous_states = [name]
130:             end
131:           end
132: 
133:           def insert_checkout_step(name, options = {})
134:             before = options.delete(:before)
135:             after = options.delete(:after) unless before
136:             after = checkout_steps.keys.last unless before || after
137: 
138:             cloned_steps = checkout_steps.clone
139:             cloned_removed_transitions = removed_transitions.clone
140:             checkout_flow do
141:               cloned_steps.each_pair do |key, value|
142:                 go_to_state(name, options) if key == before
143:                 go_to_state(key, value)
144:                 go_to_state(name, options) if key == after
145:               end
146:               cloned_removed_transitions.each do |transition|
147:                 remove_transition(transition)
148:               end
149:             end
150:           end
151: 
152:           def remove_checkout_step(name)
153:             cloned_steps = checkout_steps.clone
154:             cloned_removed_transitions = removed_transitions.clone
155:             checkout_flow do
156:               cloned_steps.each_pair do |key, value|
157:                 go_to_state(key, value) unless key == name
158:               end
159:               cloned_removed_transitions.each do |transition|
160:                 remove_transition(transition)
161:               end
162:             end
163:           end
164: 
165:           def remove_transition(options = {})
166:             removed_transitions << options
167:             next_event_transitions.delete(find_transition(options))
168:           end
169: 
170:           def find_transition(options = {})
171:             return nil if options.nil? || !options.include?(:from) || !options.include?(:to)
172: 
173:             next_event_transitions.detect do |transition|
174:               transition[options[:from].to_sym] == options[:to].to_sym
175:             end
176:           end
177: 
178:           def next_event_transitions
179:             @next_event_transitions ||= []
180:           end
181: 
182:           def checkout_steps
183:             @checkout_steps ||= {}
184:           end
185: 
186:           def checkout_step_names
187:             checkout_steps.keys
188:           end
189: 
190:           def add_transition(options)
191:             next_event_transitions << {options.delete(:from) => options.delete(:to)}.merge(options)
192:           end
193: 
194:           def removed_transitions
195:             @removed_transitions ||= []
196:           end
197:         end
198:       end
199:     end
200:   end
201: end

3: module Spree
4:   module Core
5:     class StateMachines
6:       module Order
7:         def self.included(klass)
8:           klass.extend ClassMethods
9:           klass.include StateChangeTracking
10:         end
11: 
12:         def checkout_steps
13:           steps = self.class.checkout_steps.each_with_object([]) { |(step, options), checkout_steps|
14:             next if options.include?(:if) && !options[:if].call(self)
15: 
16:             checkout_steps << step
17:           }.map(&:to_s)
18:           # Ensure there is always a complete step
19:           steps << "complete" unless steps.include?("complete")
20:           steps
21:         end
22: 
23:         def has_checkout_step?(step)
24:           step.present? && checkout_steps.include?(step)
25:         end
26: 
27:         def passed_checkout_step?(step)
28:           has_checkout_step?(step) && checkout_step_index(step) < checkout_step_index(state)
29:         end
30: 
31:         def checkout_step_index(step)
32:           checkout_steps.index(step).to_i
33:         end
34: 
35:         def can_go_to_state?(state)
36:           return false unless has_checkout_step?(self.state) && has_checkout_step?(state)
37: 
38:           checkout_step_index(state) > checkout_step_index(self.state)
39:         end
40:       end
41:     end
42:   end
43: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   class SimpleOrderContents
5:     attr_accessor :order
6: 
7:     def initialize(order)
8:       @order = order
9:     end
10: 
11:     # Add a line items to the order if there is inventory to do so
12:     # and populate Promotions
13:     #
14:     # @param [Spree::Variant] variant The variant the line_item should
15:     #   be associated with
16:     # @param [Integer] quantity The line_item quantity
17:     # @param [Hash] options Options for the adding proccess
18:     #   Valid options:
19:     #     shipment: [Spree::Shipment] LineItem target shipment
20:     #
21:     # @return [Spree::LineItem]
22:     def add(variant, quantity = 1, options = {})
23:       line_item = add_to_line_item(variant, quantity, options)
24:       after_add_or_remove(line_item, options)
25:     end
26: 
27:     def remove(variant, quantity = 1, options = {})
28:       line_item = remove_from_line_item(variant, quantity, options)
29:       after_add_or_remove(line_item, options)
30:     end
31: 
32:     def remove_line_item(line_item, options = {})
33:       order.line_items.destroy(line_item)
34:       after_add_or_remove(line_item, options)
35:     end
36: 
37:     def update_cart(params)
38:       if order.update(params)
39:         unless order.completed?
40:           order.line_items = order.line_items.select { |li| li.quantity > 0 }
41:           order.check_shipments_and_restart_checkout
42:         end
43:         reload_totals
44:         true
45:       else
46:         false
47:       end
48:     end
49: 
50:     def advance
51:       while @order.next; end
52:     end
53: 
54:     def approve(user: nil, name: nil)
55:       if user.blank? && name.blank?
56:         raise ArgumentError, "user or name must be specified"
57:       end
58: 
59:       order.update!(
60:         approver: user,
61:         approver_name: name,
62:         approved_at: Time.current
63:       )
64:     end
65: 
66:     private
67: 
68:     def after_add_or_remove(line_item, options = {})
69:       shipment = options[:shipment]
70:       shipment.present? ? shipment.update_amounts : order.check_shipments_and_restart_checkout
71:       reload_totals
72:       line_item
73:     end
74: 
75:     def reload_totals
76:       @order.recalculate
77:     end
78: 
79:     def add_to_line_item(variant, quantity, options = {})
80:       line_item = grab_line_item_by_variant(variant, false, options)
81: 
82:       line_item ||= order.line_items.new(
83:         quantity: 0,
84:         variant:,
85:         adjustments: []
86:       )
87: 
88:       permitted_attributes = Spree::PermittedAttributes.line_item_attributes.dup
89:       permitted_attributes << {admin_metadata: {}} if options[:admin_metadata].present?
90: 
91:       line_item.quantity += quantity.to_i
92:       line_item.options = ActionController::Parameters.new(options).permit(permitted_attributes).to_h
93: 
94:       line_item.target_shipment = options[:shipment]
95:       line_item.save!
96:       line_item
97:     end
98: 
99:     def remove_from_line_item(variant, quantity, options = {})
100:       line_item = grab_line_item_by_variant(variant, true, options)
101:       line_item.quantity -= quantity
102:       line_item.target_shipment = options[:shipment]
103: 
104:       if line_item.quantity <= 0
105:         order.line_items.destroy(line_item)
106:       else
107:         line_item.save!
108:       end
109: 
110:       line_item
111:     end
112: 
113:     def grab_line_item_by_variant(variant, raise_error = false, options = {})
114:       line_item = order.find_line_item_by_variant(variant, options)
115: 
116:       if line_item.blank? && raise_error
117:         raise ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound, "Line item not found for variant #{variant.sku}"
118:       end
119: 
120:       line_item
121:     end
122:   end
123: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   # Variants placed in the Order at a particular price.
5:   #
6:   # `Spree::LineItem` is an ActiveRecord model which records which `Spree::Variant`
7:   # a customer has chosen to place in their order. It also acts as the permanent
8:   # record of the customer's order by recording relevant price, taxation, and inventory
9:   # concerns. Line items can also have adjustments placed on them as part of the
10:   # promotion system.
11:   #
12:   class LineItem < Spree::Base
13:     include Metadata
14: 
15:     belongs_to :order, class_name: "Spree::Order", inverse_of: :line_items, touch: true, optional: true
16:     belongs_to :variant, -> { with_discarded }, class_name: "Spree::Variant", inverse_of: :line_items, optional: true
17:     belongs_to :tax_category, class_name: "Spree::TaxCategory", optional: true
18: 
19:     has_one :product, through: :variant
20: 
21:     has_many :adjustments, as: :adjustable, inverse_of: :adjustable, dependent: :destroy, autosave: true
22:     has_many :inventory_units, inverse_of: :line_item
23: 
24:     before_validation :normalize_quantity
25:     after_initialize :set_required_attributes
26: 
27:     validates :variant, presence: true
28:     validates :quantity, numericality: {
29:       only_integer: true,
30:       greater_than: -1
31:     }
32:     validates :price, numericality: true
33:     validate :price_match_order_currency
34: 
35:     after_save :update_inventory
36: 
37:     before_destroy :update_inventory
38:     before_destroy :destroy_inventory_units
39: 
40:     delegate :name, :description, :sku, :should_track_inventory?, to: :variant
41:     delegate :tax_category, :tax_category_id, to: :variant, prefix: true
42:     delegate :currency, to: :order, allow_nil: true
43: 
44:     attr_accessor :target_shipment, :price_currency
45: 
46:     self.allowed_ransackable_associations = ["variant"]
47:     self.allowed_ransackable_attributes = ["variant_id"]
48: 
49:     # @return [BigDecimal] the amount of this line item, which is the line
50:     #   item's price multiplied by its quantity.
51:     def amount
52:       price * quantity
53:     end
54:     alias_method :subtotal, :amount
55: 
56:     # @return [BigDecimal] the amount of this line item, taking into
57:     #   consideration all its adjustments.
58:     def total
59:       amount + adjustment_total
60:     end
61: 
62:     # @return [BigDecimal] the amount of this item, taking into consideration
63:     #   all non-tax adjustments.
64:     def total_before_tax
65:       amount + adjustments.reject(&:tax?).sum(&:amount)
66:     end
67: 
68:     # @return [BigDecimal] the amount of this line item before VAT tax
69:     # @note just like `amount`, this does not include any additional tax
70:     def total_excluding_vat
71:       total_before_tax - included_tax_total
72:     end
73: 
74:     extend Spree::DisplayMoney
75: 
76:     money_methods :amount, :price,
77:       :included_tax_total, :additional_tax_total,
78:       :total, :total_before_tax, :total_excluding_vat
79: 
80:     # @return [Spree::Money] the price of this line item
81:     alias_method :money_price, :display_price
82:     alias_method :single_display_amount, :display_price
83:     alias_method :single_money, :display_price
84: 
85:     # @return [Spree::Money] the amount of this line item
86:     alias_method :money, :display_amount
87: 
88:     # Sets price from a `Spree::Money` object
89:     #
90:     # @param [Spree::Money] money - the money object to obtain price from
91:     def money_price=(money)
92:       if !money
93:         self.price = nil
94:       else
95:         self.price_currency = money.currency.iso_code
96:         self.price = money.to_d
97:       end
98:     end
99: 
100:     # @return [Boolean] true when it is possible to supply the required
101:     #   quantity of stock of this line item's variant
102:     def sufficient_stock?
103:       Spree::Config.stock.quantifier_class.new(variant).can_supply? quantity
104:     end
105: 
106:     # @return [Boolean] true when it is not possible to supply the required
107:     #   quantity of stock of this line item's variant
108:     def insufficient_stock?
109:       !sufficient_stock?
110:     end
111: 
112:     # Sets options on the line item and updates the price.
113:     #
114:     # The options can be arbitrary attributes on the LineItem.
115:     #
116:     # @param options [Hash] options for this line item
117:     def options=(options = {})
118:       return unless options.present?
119: 
120:       assign_attributes options
121: 
122:       # When price is part of the options we are not going to fetch
123:       # it from the variant. Please note that this always allows to set
124:       # a price for this line item, even if there is no existing price
125:       # for the associated line item in the order currency.
126:       unless options.key?(:price) || options.key?("price")
127:         self.money_price = variant.price_for_options(pricing_options)&.money
128:       end
129:     end
130: 
131:     # Recalculate the price using the pricing options for this line item.
132:     # Useful for making sure carts are up-to-date when prices change.
133:     # Will not make changes to completed orders.
134:     #
135:     def recalculate_price
136:       return if order.completed?
137:       self.money_price = variant.price_for_options(pricing_options)&.money
138:     end
139: 
140:     def pricing_options
141:       Spree::Config.pricing_options_class.from_line_item(self)
142:     end
143: 
144:     # @return [Spree::TaxCategory] the variant's tax category
145:     #
146:     # This returns the variant's tax category if the tax category ID on the line_item is nil. It looks
147:     # like an association, but really is an override.
148:     #
149:     def tax_category
150:       super || variant_tax_category
151:     end
152: 
153:     # @return [Integer] the variant's tax category ID
154:     #
155:     # This returns the variant's tax category ID if the tax category ID on the line_id is nil. It looks
156:     # like an association, but really is an override.
157:     #
158:     def tax_category_id
159:       super || variant_tax_category_id
160:     end
161: 
162:     private
163: 
164:     # Sets the quantity to zero if it is nil or less than zero.
165:     def normalize_quantity
166:       self.quantity = 0 if quantity.nil? || quantity < 0
167:     end
168: 
169:     # Sets tax category, price-related attributes from
170:     # its variant if they are nil and a variant is present.
171:     def set_required_attributes
172:       return if persisted?
173:       return unless variant
174:       self.tax_category ||= variant.tax_category
175:       set_pricing_attributes
176:     end
177: 
178:     # Set price, cost_price and currency.
179:     def set_pricing_attributes
180:       self.cost_price ||= variant.cost_price
181:       self.money_price = variant.price_for_options(pricing_options)&.money if price.nil?
182:       true
183:     end
184: 
185:     def update_inventory
186:       if (saved_changes? || target_shipment.present?) && order.has_checkout_step?("delivery")
187:         Spree::OrderInventory.new(order, self).verify(target_shipment)
188:       end
189:     end
190: 
191:     def destroy_inventory_units
192:       inventory_units.destroy_all
193:     end
194: 
195:     def price_match_order_currency
196:       return if price_currency.blank? || price_currency == currency
197: 
198:       errors.add(:price, :does_not_match_order_currency)
199:     end
200:   end
201: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   # An order's planned shipments including tracking and cost.
5:   #
6:   class Shipment < Spree::Base
7:     include Metadata
8: 
9:     belongs_to :order, class_name: "Spree::Order", touch: true, inverse_of: :shipments, optional: true
10:     belongs_to :stock_location, class_name: "Spree::StockLocation", optional: true
11: 
12:     has_many :adjustments, as: :adjustable, inverse_of: :adjustable, dependent: :delete_all, autosave: true
13:     has_many :inventory_units, dependent: :destroy, inverse_of: :shipment
14:     has_many :shipping_rates, -> { order(:cost) }, dependent: :destroy, inverse_of: :shipment, autosave: true
15:     has_many :shipping_methods, through: :shipping_rates
16:     has_many :state_changes, as: :stateful
17:     has_many :cartons, -> { distinct }, through: :inventory_units
18:     has_many :line_items, -> { distinct }, through: :inventory_units
19: 
20:     before_validation :set_cost_zero_when_nil
21: 
22:     before_destroy :ensure_can_destroy
23: 
24:     # TODO: remove the suppress_mailer temporary variable once we are calling 'ship'
25:     # from outside of the state machine and can actually pass variables through.
26:     attr_accessor :special_instructions, :suppress_mailer
27: 
28:     accepts_nested_attributes_for :inventory_units
29: 
30:     make_permalink field: :number, length: 11, prefix: "H"
31: 
32:     scope :pending, -> { with_state("pending") }
33:     scope :ready, -> { with_state("ready") }
34:     scope :shipped, -> { with_state("shipped") }
35:     scope :trackable, -> { where("tracking IS NOT NULL AND tracking != ''") }
36:     scope :with_state, ->(*state) { where(state:) }
37:     # sort by most recent shipped_at, falling back to created_at. add "id desc" to make specs that involve this scope more deterministic.
38:     scope :reverse_chronological, -> {
39:       order(Arel.sql("coalesce(#{Spree::Shipment.table_name}.shipped_at, #{Spree::Shipment.table_name}.created_at) desc"), id: :desc)
40:     }
41: 
42:     scope :by_store, ->(store) { joins(:order).merge(Spree::Order.by_store(store)) }
43: 
44:     include ::Spree::Config.state_machines.shipment
45: 
46:     self.allowed_ransackable_associations = ["order"]
47:     self.allowed_ransackable_attributes = ["number"]
48: 
49:     delegate :tax_category, :tax_category_id, to: :selected_shipping_rate, allow_nil: true
50: 
51:     def can_transition_from_pending_to_shipped?
52:       !requires_shipment?
53:     end
54: 
55:     def can_transition_from_pending_to_ready?
56:       order.can_ship? &&
57:         inventory_units.all? { |iu| iu.shipped? || iu.allow_ship? || iu.canceled? } &&
58:         (order.paid? || !Spree::Config[:require_payment_to_ship])
59:     end
60: 
61:     def can_transition_from_canceled_to_ready?
62:       can_transition_from_pending_to_ready?
63:     end
64: 
65:     extend DisplayMoney
66: 
67:     money_methods(
68:       :cost, :amount, :item_cost,
69:       :total, :total_before_tax
70:     )
71:     alias_attribute :amount, :cost
72: 
73:     def after_cancel
74:       manifest.each { |item| manifest_restock(item) }
75:     end
76: 
77:     def after_resume
78:       manifest.each { |item| manifest_unstock(item) }
79:     end
80: 
81:     def backordered?
82:       inventory_units.any?(&:backordered?)
83:     end
84: 
85:     def currency
86:       order ? order.currency : Spree::Config[:currency]
87:     end
88: 
89:     # @return [BigDecimal] the amount of this shipment, taking into
90:     #   consideration all its adjustments.
91:     def total
92:       cost + adjustment_total
93:     end
94: 
95:     # @return [BigDecimal] the amount of this item, taking into consideration
96:     #   all non-tax adjustments.
97:     def total_before_tax
98:       amount + adjustments.reject(&:tax?).sum(&:amount)
99:     end
100: 
101:     # @return [BigDecimal] the amount of this shipment before VAT tax
102:     # @note just like `cost`, this does not include any additional tax
103:     def total_excluding_vat
104:       total_before_tax - included_tax_total
105:     end
106: 
107:     def total_with_items
108:       total + item_cost
109:     end
110: 
111:     def editable_by?(_user)
112:       !shipped?
113:     end
114: 
115:     # Decrement the stock counts for all pending inventory units in this
116:     # shipment and mark.
117:     # Any previous non-pending inventory units are skipped as their stock had
118:     # already been allocated.
119:     def finalize!
120:       finalize_pending_inventory_units
121:     end
122: 
123:     def include?(variant)
124:       inventory_units_for(variant).present?
125:     end
126: 
127:     def inventory_units_for(variant)
128:       inventory_units.where(variant_id: variant.id)
129:     end
130: 
131:     def inventory_units_for_item(line_item, variant = nil)
132:       inventory_units.where(line_item_id: line_item.id, variant_id: line_item.variant.id || variant.id)
133:     end
134: 
135:     def item_cost
136:       line_items.sum(&:total)
137:     end
138: 
139:     def ready_or_pending?
140:       ready? || pending?
141:     end
142: 
143:     def refresh_rates
144:       return shipping_rates if shipped?
145:       return [] unless can_get_rates?
146: 
147:       # StockEstimator.new assigment below will replace the current shipping_method
148:       original_shipping_method_id = shipping_method.try!(:id)
149: 
150:       new_rates = Spree::Config.stock.estimator_class.new.shipping_rates(to_package)
151: 
152:       # If one of the new rates matches the previously selected shipping
153:       # method, select that instead of the default provided by the estimator.
154:       # Otherwise, keep the default.
155:       selected_rate = new_rates.detect { |rate| rate.shipping_method_id == original_shipping_method_id }
156:       if selected_rate
157:         new_rates.each do |rate|
158:           rate.selected = (rate == selected_rate)
159:         end
160:       end
161: 
162:       self.shipping_rates = new_rates
163:       save!
164: 
165:       shipping_rates
166:     end
167: 
168:     def select_shipping_method(shipping_method)
169:       estimator = Spree::Config.stock.estimator_class.new
170:       rates = estimator.shipping_rates(to_package, false)
171:       rate = rates.detect { |detected| detected.shipping_method_id == shipping_method.id }
172:       rate.selected = true
173:       self.shipping_rates = [rate]
174:     end
175: 
176:     def selected_shipping_rate
177:       shipping_rates.detect(&:selected?)
178:     end
179: 
180:     def manifest
181:       @manifest ||= Spree::ShippingManifest.new(inventory_units:).items
182:     end
183: 
184:     def selected_shipping_rate_id
185:       selected_shipping_rate.try(:id)
186:     end
187: 
188:     def selected_shipping_rate_id=(id)
189:       return if selected_shipping_rate_id == id
190:       new_rate = shipping_rates.detect { |rate| rate.id == id.to_i }
191:       unless new_rate
192:         fail(
193:           ArgumentError,
194:           "Could not find shipping rate id #{id} for shipment #{number}"
195:         )
196:       end
197: 
198:       transaction do
199:         selected_shipping_rate&.update!(selected: false)
200:         new_rate.update!(selected: true)
201:       end
202:     end
203: 
204:     def determine_state(order)
205:       Spree.deprecator.warn "Use Spree::Shipment#recalculate_state instead"
206: 
207:       return "shipped" if shipped?
208:       return "canceled" if order.canceled? || inventory_units.all?(&:canceled?)
209:       return "pending" unless order.can_ship?
210:       if can_transition_from_pending_to_ready?
211:         "ready"
212:       else
213:         "pending"
214:       end
215:     end
216: 
217:     # Assigns the appropriate +state+ according to the following logic:
218:     #
219:     # canceled   if order is canceled
220:     # pending    unless order is complete and +order.payment_state+ is +paid+
221:     # shipped    if already shipped (ie. does not change the state)
222:     # ready      all other cases
223:     def recalculate_state
224:       self.state =
225:         if shipped?
226:           "shipped"
227:         elsif inventory_units.none?
228:           "pending"
229:         elsif order.canceled? || inventory_units.all?(&:canceled?)
230:           "canceled"
231:         elsif !order.can_ship?
232:           "pending"
233:         elsif can_transition_from_pending_to_ready?
234:           "ready"
235:         else
236:           "pending"
237:         end
238:     end
239: 
240:     def set_up_inventory(state, variant, _order, line_item)
241:       inventory_units.create(
242:         state:,
243:         variant_id: variant.id,
244:         line_item_id: line_item.id
245:       )
246:     end
247: 
248:     def shipped=(value)
249:       return unless value == "1" && shipped_at.nil?
250:       self.shipped_at = Time.current
251:     end
252: 
253:     def shipping_method
254:       selected_shipping_rate.try(:shipping_method)
255:     end
256: 
257:     # Only one of either included_tax_total or additional_tax_total is set
258:     # This method returns the total of the two. Saves having to check if
259:     # tax is included or additional.
260:     def tax_total
261:       included_tax_total + additional_tax_total
262:     end
263: 
264:     def to_package
265:       package = Stock::Package.new(stock_location)
266:       package.shipment = self
267:       inventory_units.includes(variant: :product).joins(:variant).group_by(&:state).each do |state, state_inventory_units|
268:         package.add_multiple state_inventory_units, state.to_sym
269:       end
270:       package
271:     end
272: 
273:     def to_param
274:       number
275:     end
276: 
277:     def tracking_url
278:       return nil unless tracking && shipping_method
279: 
280:       @tracking_url ||= shipping_method.build_tracking_url(tracking)
281:     end
282: 
283:     def update_amounts
284:       assign_amounts
285:       persist_amounts
286:     end
287: 
288:     def assign_amounts
289:       return unless selected_shipping_rate
290:       self.cost = selected_shipping_rate.cost
291:     end
292: 
293:     def persist_amounts
294:       if cost_changed?
295:         update_columns(
296:           cost:,
297:           updated_at: Time.current
298:         )
299:       end
300:     end
301: 
302:     # Update Shipment and make sure Order states follow the shipment changes
303:     def update_attributes_and_order(params = {})
304:       if update(params)
305:         if params.key? :selected_shipping_rate_id
306:           # Changing the selected Shipping Rate won't update the cost (for now)
307:           # so we persist the Shipment#cost before running `order.recalculate`
308:           update_amounts
309:           order.recalculate
310:         end
311: 
312:         true
313:       end
314:     end
315: 
316:     # Updates the state of the Shipment bypassing any callbacks.
317:     #
318:     # If this moves the shipment to the 'shipped' state, after_ship will be
319:     # called.
320:     def update_state
321:       old_state = state
322:       new_state = recalculate_state
323:       if new_state != old_state
324:         update_columns state: new_state, updated_at: Time.current
325:         after_ship if new_state == "shipped"
326:       end
327:     end
328: 
329:     def requires_shipment?
330:       !stock_location || stock_location.fulfillable?
331:     end
332: 
333:     private
334: 
335:     def finalize_pending_inventory_units
336:       pending_units = inventory_units.select(&:pending?)
337:       Spree::Stock::InventoryUnitsFinalizer.new(pending_units).run!
338:     end
339: 
340:     def after_ship
341:       order.shipping.ship_shipment(self, suppress_mailer:)
342:     end
343: 
344:     def can_get_rates?
345:       order.ship_address&.valid?
346:     end
347: 
348:     def manifest_restock(item)
349:       if item.states["on_hand"].to_i > 0
350:         stock_location.restock item.variant, item.states["on_hand"], self
351:       end
352: 
353:       if item.states["backordered"].to_i > 0
354:         stock_location.restock_backordered item.variant, item.states["backordered"]
355:       end
356:     end
357: 
358:     def manifest_unstock(item)
359:       stock_location.unstock item.variant, item.quantity, self
360:     end
361: 
362:     def set_cost_zero_when_nil
363:       self.cost = 0 unless cost
364:     end
365: 
366:     def ensure_can_destroy
367:       if shipped? || canceled?
368:         errors.add(:state, :cannot_destroy, state:)
369:         throw :abort
370:       end
371:     end
372:   end
373: end
