1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   # Manage and process a payment for an order, from a specific
5:   # source (e.g. `Spree::CreditCard`) using a specific payment method (e.g
6:   # `Solidus::Gateway::Braintree`).
7:   #
8:   class Payment < Spree::Base
9:     include Spree::Payment::Processing
10:     include Metadata
11: 
12:     IDENTIFIER_CHARS = (("A".."Z").to_a + ("0".."9").to_a - %w[0 1 I O]).freeze
13:     NON_RISKY_AVS_CODES = ["B", "D", "H", "J", "M", "Q", "T", "V", "X", "Y"].freeze
14:     RISKY_AVS_CODES = ["A", "C", "E", "F", "G", "I", "K", "L", "N", "O", "P", "R", "S", "U", "W", "Z"].freeze
15: 
16:     belongs_to :order, class_name: "Spree::Order", touch: true, inverse_of: :payments, optional: true
17:     belongs_to :source, polymorphic: true, optional: true
18:     belongs_to :payment_method, -> { with_discarded }, class_name: "Spree::PaymentMethod", inverse_of: :payments, optional: true
19: 
20:     has_many :log_entries, as: :source
21:     has_many :state_changes, as: :stateful
22:     has_many :capture_events, class_name: "Spree::PaymentCaptureEvent"
23:     has_many :refunds, inverse_of: :payment
24: 
25:     before_validation :validate_source, unless: :invalid?
26:     before_create :set_unique_identifier
27: 
28:     after_save :create_payment_profile, if: :profiles_supported?
29: 
30:     # update the order totals, etc.
31:     after_save :update_order
32: 
33:     after_create :create_eligible_credit_event
34: 
35:     # invalidate previously entered payments
36:     after_create :invalidate_old_payments
37: 
38:     attr_accessor :request_env
39: 
40:     validates :amount, numericality: true
41:     validates :source, presence: true, if: :source_required?
42:     validates :payment_method, presence: true
43: 
44:     default_scope -> { order(:created_at) }
45: 
46:     scope :from_credit_card, -> { where(source_type: "Spree::CreditCard") }
47:     scope :with_state, ->(state) { where(state: state.to_s) }
48: 
49:     scope :checkout, -> { with_state("checkout") }
50:     scope :completed, -> { with_state("completed") }
51:     scope :pending, -> { with_state("pending") }
52:     scope :processing, -> { with_state("processing") }
53:     scope :failed, -> { with_state("failed") }
54: 
55:     scope :risky, -> { failed.or(where(avs_response: RISKY_AVS_CODES)).or(where.not(cvv_response_code: [nil, "", "M"])) }
56:     scope :valid, -> { where.not(state: %w[failed invalid void]) }
57: 
58:     scope :store_credits, -> { where(source_type: Spree::StoreCredit.to_s) }
59:     scope :not_store_credits, -> { where(arel_table[:source_type].not_eq(Spree::StoreCredit.to_s).or(arel_table[:source_type].eq(nil))) }
60: 
61:     include ::Spree::Config.state_machines.payment
62: 
63:     # @return [String] this payment's response code
64:     def transaction_id
65:       response_code
66:     end
67: 
68:     # @return [String] this payment's currency
69:     delegate :currency, to: :order
70: 
71:     # @return [Spree::Money] this amount of this payment as money object
72:     def money
73:       Spree::Money.new(amount, {currency:})
74:     end
75:     alias_method :display_amount, :money
76: 
77:     # Sets the amount, parsing it based on i18n settings if it is a string.
78:     #
79:     # @param amount [BigDecimal, String] the desired new amount
80:     def amount=(amount)
81:       self[:amount] =
82:         case amount
83:         when String
84:           separator = I18n.t("number.currency.format.separator")
85:           number = amount.delete("^0-9-#{separator}.").tr(separator, ".")
86:           number.presence&.to_d
87:         end || amount
88:     end
89: 
90:     # The total amount this payment can be credited.
91:     #
92:     # @return [BigDecimal] the amount of this payment minus refunds
93:     def credit_allowed
94:       amount - refunds.sum(:amount)
95:     end
96: 
97:     # @return [Boolean] true when this payment can be credited
98:     def can_credit?
99:       credit_allowed > 0
100:     end
101: 
102:     # @return [Boolean] true when this payment has been fully refunded
103:     def fully_refunded?
104:       refunds.map(&:amount).sum == amount
105:     end
106: 
107:     # @return [Array<String>] the actions available on this payment
108:     def actions
109:       sa = source_actions
110:       sa |= ["failure"] if processing?
111:       sa
112:     end
113: 
114:     # @return [Object] the source of ths payment
115:     def payment_source
116:       res = source.is_a?(Payment) ? source.source : source
117:       res || payment_method
118:     end
119: 
120:     # @return [Boolean] true when this payment is risky
121:     def risky?
122:       is_avs_risky? || is_cvv_risky? || state == "failed"
123:     end
124: 
125:     # @return [Boolean] true when this payment is risky based on address
126:     def is_avs_risky?
127:       return false if avs_response.blank? || NON_RISKY_AVS_CODES.include?(avs_response)
128:       true
129:     end
130: 
131:     # @return [Boolean] true when this payment is risky based on cvv
132:     def is_cvv_risky?
133:       return false if cvv_response_code == "M"
134:       return false if cvv_response_code.nil?
135:       true
136:     end
137: 
138:     # @return [BigDecimal] the total amount captured on this payment
139:     def captured_amount
140:       capture_events.sum(:amount)
141:     end
142: 
143:     # @return [BigDecimal] the total amount left uncaptured on this payment
144:     def uncaptured_amount
145:       amount - captured_amount
146:     end
147: 
148:     # @return [Boolean] true when the payment method exists and is a store credit payment method
149:     def store_credit?
150:       payment_method.try!(:store_credit?)
151:     end
152: 
153:     private
154: 
155:     def source_actions
156:       return [] unless payment_source&.respond_to?(:actions)
157:       payment_source.actions.select { |action| !payment_source.respond_to?("can_#{action}?") || payment_source.send("can_#{action}?", self) }
158:     end
159: 
160:     def validate_source
161:       if source && !source.valid?
162:         source.errors.each do |error|
163:           field_name = I18n.t("activerecord.attributes.#{source.class.to_s.underscore}.#{error.attribute}")
164:           errors.add(I18n.t(source.class.to_s.demodulize.underscore, scope: "spree"), "#{field_name} #{error.message}")
165:         end
166:       end
167:       if errors.any?
168:         throw :abort
169:       end
170:     end
171: 
172:     def source_required?
173:       !!payment_method&.source_required?
174:     end
175: 
176:     def profiles_supported?
177:       !!payment_method.try(:payment_profiles_supported?)
178:     end
179: 
180:     def create_payment_profile
181:       # Don't attempt to create on bad payments.
182:       return if %w[invalid failed].include?(state)
183:       # Payment profile cannot be created without source
184:       return unless source
185:       # Imported payments shouldn't create a payment profile.
186:       return if source.imported
187: 
188:       payment_method.create_profile(self)
189:     rescue ActiveMerchant::ConnectionError => error
190:       gateway_error error
191:     end
192: 
193:     def invalidate_old_payments
194:       if !store_credit? && !["invalid", "failed"].include?(state)
195:         order.payments.select { |payment|
196:           payment.state == "checkout" && !payment.store_credit? && payment.id != id
197:         }.each(&:invalidate!)
198:       end
199:     end
200: 
201:     def update_order
202:       if order.completed? || completed? || void?
203:         order.recalculate
204:       end
205:     end
206: 
207:     # Necessary because some payment gateways will refuse payments with
208:     # duplicate IDs. We *were* using the Order number, but that's set once and
209:     # is unchanging. What we need is a unique identifier on a per-payment basis,
210:     # and this is it. Related to https://github.com/spree/spree/issues/1998.
211:     # See https://github.com/spree/spree/issues/1998#issuecomment-12869105
212:     def set_unique_identifier
213:       loop do
214:         self.number = generate_identifier
215:         break unless self.class.exists?(number:)
216:       end
217:     end
218: 
219:     def generate_identifier
220:       Array.new(8) { IDENTIFIER_CHARS.sample }.join
221:     end
222: 
223:     def create_eligible_credit_event
224:       # When cancelling an order, a payment with the negative amount
225:       # of the payment total is created to refund the customer. That
226:       # payment has a source of itself (Spree::Payment) no matter the
227:       # type of payment getting refunded, hence the additional check
228:       # if the source is a store credit.
229:       if store_credit? && source.is_a?(Spree::StoreCredit)
230:         source.update!({
231:           action: Spree::StoreCredit::ELIGIBLE_ACTION,
232:           action_amount: amount,
233:           action_authorization_code: response_code
234:         })
235:       end
236:     end
237:   end
238: end

(End of file - total 238 lines)
</content>

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   # Adjustments represent a change to the +item_total+ of an Order. Each
5:   # adjustment has an +amount+ that can be either positive or negative.
6:   #
7:   # Adjustments can be "unfinalized" or "finalized". Once an adjustment is finalized, it
8:   # will not be automatically updated.
9:   class Adjustment < Spree::Base
10:     belongs_to :adjustable, polymorphic: true, touch: true, optional: true
11:     belongs_to :source, polymorphic: true, optional: true
12:     belongs_to :order, class_name: "Spree::Order", inverse_of: false, optional: true
13:     belongs_to :adjustment_reason, class_name: "Spree::AdjustmentReason", inverse_of: :adjustments, optional: true
14: 
15:     validates :adjustable, presence: true
16:     validates :order, presence: true
17:     validates :label, presence: true
18:     validates :amount, numericality: true
19: 
20:     scope :not_finalized, -> { where(finalized: false) }
21:     scope :finalized, -> { where(finalized: true) }
22:     scope :cancellation, -> { where(source_type: "Spree::UnitCancel") }
23:     scope :tax, -> { where(source_type: "Spree::TaxRate") }
24:     scope :non_tax, -> do
25:       source_type = arel_table[:source_type]
26:       where(source_type.not_eq("Spree::TaxRate").or(source_type.eq(nil)))
27:     end
28:     scope :price, -> { where(adjustable_type: "Spree::LineItem") }
29:     scope :shipping, -> { where(adjustable_type: "Spree::Shipment") }
30:     scope :eligible, -> { all }
31:     class << self
32:       deprecate :eligible, deprecator: Spree.deprecator
33:     end
34:     scope :charge, -> { where("#{quoted_table_name}.amount >= 0") }
35:     scope :credit, -> { where("#{quoted_table_name}.amount < 0") }
36:     scope :nonzero, -> { where("#{quoted_table_name}.amount != 0") }
37:     scope :promotion, -> { where(source_type: Spree::Config.adjustment_promotion_source_types.map(&:to_s)) }
38:     scope :non_promotion, -> { where.not(source_type: Spree::Config.adjustment_promotion_source_types.map(&:to_s)) }
39:     scope :return_authorization, -> { where(source_type: "Spree::ReturnAuthorization") }
40:     scope :is_included, -> { where(included: true) }
41:     scope :additional, -> { where(included: false) }
42: 
43:     singleton_class.deprecate :return_authorization, deprecator: Spree.deprecator
44: 
45:     allowed_ransackable_attributes << "label"
46: 
47:     extend DisplayMoney
48: 
49:     money_methods :amount
50: 
51:     def finalize!
52:       update!(finalized: true)
53:     end
54: 
55:     def unfinalize!
56:       update!(finalized: false)
57:     end
58: 
59:     def finalize
60:       update(finalized: true)
61:     end
62: 
63:     def unfinalize
64:       update(finalized: false)
65:     end
66: 
67:     def currency
68:       adjustable ? adjustable.currency : Spree::Config[:currency]
69:     end
70: 
71:     # @return [Boolean] true when this is a promotion adjustment (Promotion adjustments have a {PromotionAction} source)
72:     def promotion?
73:       source_type.to_s.in?(Spree::Config.adjustment_promotion_source_types.map(&:to_s))
74:     end
75: 
76:     # @return [Boolean] true when this is a tax adjustment (Tax adjustments have a {TaxRate} source)
77:     def tax?
78:       source_type == "Spree::TaxRate"
79:     end
80: 
81:     # @return [Boolean] true when this is a cancellation adjustment (Cancellation adjustments have a {UnitCancel} source)
82:     def cancellation?
83:       source_type == "Spree::UnitCancel"
84:     end
85: 
86:     def eligible?
87:       true
88:     end
89:     alias_method :eligible, :eligible?
90:   end
91: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   # Tracks the state of line items' fulfillment.
5:   #
6:   class InventoryUnit < Spree::Base
7:     PRE_SHIPMENT_STATES = %w[backordered on_hand]
8:     POST_SHIPMENT_STATES = %w[returned]
9:     CANCELABLE_STATES = ["on_hand", "backordered", "shipped"]
10: 
11:     belongs_to :variant, -> { with_discarded }, class_name: "Spree::Variant", inverse_of: :inventory_units, optional: true
12:     belongs_to :shipment, class_name: "Spree::Shipment", touch: true, inverse_of: :inventory_units, optional: true
13:     belongs_to :carton, class_name: "Spree::Carton", inverse_of: :inventory_units, optional: true
14:     belongs_to :line_item, class_name: "Spree::LineItem", inverse_of: :inventory_units, optional: true
15: 
16:     has_many :return_items, inverse_of: :inventory_unit, dependent: :destroy
17:     has_one :original_return_item, class_name: "Spree::ReturnItem", foreign_key: :exchange_inventory_unit_id, dependent: :destroy
18:     has_one :unit_cancel, class_name: "Spree::UnitCancel"
19:     has_one :order, through: :shipment
20: 
21:     delegate :order_id, to: :shipment
22: 
23:     def order=(_)
24:       raise "The order association has been removed from InventoryUnit. The order is now determined from the shipment."
25:     end
26: 
27:     validates :shipment, :line_item, :variant, presence: true
28: 
29:     before_destroy :ensure_can_destroy
30: 
31:     scope :pending, -> { where pending: true }
32:     scope :backordered, -> { where state: "backordered" }
33:     scope :on_hand, -> { where state: "on_hand" }
34:     scope :pre_shipment, -> { where(state: PRE_SHIPMENT_STATES) }
35:     scope :shipped, -> { where state: "shipped" }
36:     scope :post_shipment, -> { where(state: POST_SHIPMENT_STATES) }
37:     scope :returned, -> { where state: "returned" }
38:     scope :canceled, -> { where(state: "canceled") }
39:     scope :not_canceled, -> { where.not(state: "canceled") }
40:     scope :cancelable, -> { where(state: Spree::InventoryUnit::CANCELABLE_STATES, pending: false) }
41:     scope :backordered_per_variant, ->(stock_item) do
42:       includes(:shipment, :order)
43:         .where("spree_shipments.state != 'canceled'").references(:shipment)
44:         .where(variant_id: stock_item.variant_id)
45:         .where("spree_orders.completed_at is not null")
46:         .backordered.order(Spree::Order.arel_table[:completed_at].asc)
47:     end
48: 
49:     # @method backordered_for_stock_item(stock_item)
50:     # @param stock_item [Spree::StockItem] the stock item of the desired
51:     #   inventory units
52:     # @return [ActiveRecord::Relation<Spree::InventoryUnit>] backordered
53:     #   inventory units for the given stock item
54:     scope :backordered_for_stock_item, ->(stock_item) do
55:       backordered_per_variant(stock_item)
56:         .where(spree_shipments: {stock_location_id: stock_item.stock_location_id})
57:     end
58: 
59:     scope :shippable, -> { on_hand }
60: 
61:     include ::Spree::Config.state_machines.inventory_unit
62: 
63:     # @return [Spree::StockItem] the first stock item from this shipment's
64:     #   stock location that is associated with this inventory unit's variant
65:     def find_stock_item
66:       Spree::StockItem.where(stock_location_id: shipment.stock_location_id,
67:         variant_id:).first
68:     end
69: 
70:     # @return [Spree::ReturnItem] a valid return item for this inventory unit
71:     #   if one exists, or a new one if one does not
72:     def current_or_new_return_item
73:       Spree::ReturnItem.from_inventory_unit(self)
74:     end
75: 
76:     # @return [BigDecimal] the portion of the additional tax on the line item
77:     #   this inventory unit belongs to that is associated with this individual
78:     #   inventory unit
79:     def additional_tax_total
80:       line_item.additional_tax_total * percentage_of_line_item
81:     end
82: 
83:     # @return [BigDecimal] the portion of the included tax on the line item
84:     #   this inventory unit belongs to that is associated with this
85:     #   individual inventory unit
86:     def included_tax_total
87:       line_item.included_tax_total * percentage_of_line_item
88:     end
89: 
90:     # @return [Boolean] true if this inventory unit has any return items
91:     #   which have requested exchanges
92:     def exchange_requested?
93:       return_items.not_expired.any?(&:exchange_requested?)
94:     end
95: 
96:     def allow_ship?
97:       on_hand?
98:     end
99: 
100:     private
101: 
102:     def fulfill_order
103:       reload
104:       order.fulfill!
105:     end
106: 
107:     def percentage_of_line_item
108:       1 / BigDecimal(line_item.quantity)
109:     end
110: 
111:     def current_return_item
112:       return_items.not_cancelled.first
113:     end
114: 
115:     def ensure_can_destroy
116:       if !backordered? && !on_hand?
117:         errors.add(:state, :cannot_destroy, state:)
118:         throw :abort
119:       end
120: 
121:       if shipment.shipped? || shipment.canceled?
122:         errors.add(:base, :cannot_destroy_shipment_state, state: shipment.state)
123:         throw :abort
124:       end
125:     end
126:   end
127: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   # Models the return of Inventory Units to a Stock Location for an Order.
5:   #
6:   class ReturnAuthorization < Spree::Base
7:     include Metadata
8: 
9:     belongs_to :order, class_name: "Spree::Order", inverse_of: :return_authorizations, optional: true
10: 
11:     has_many :return_items, inverse_of: :return_authorization, dependent: :destroy
12:     has_many :inventory_units, through: :return_items, dependent: :nullify
13:     has_many :customer_returns, through: :return_items
14: 
15:     belongs_to :stock_location, optional: true
16:     belongs_to :reason, class_name: "Spree::ReturnReason", foreign_key: :return_reason_id, optional: true
17: 
18:     before_create :generate_number
19: 
20:     accepts_nested_attributes_for :return_items, allow_destroy: true
21: 
22:     validates :order, presence: true
23:     validates :stock_location, presence: true
24:     validate :must_have_shipped_units, on: :create
25:     validate :no_previously_exchanged_inventory_units, on: :create
26: 
27:     include ::Spree::Config.state_machines.return_authorization
28: 
29:     extend DisplayMoney
30: 
31:     money_methods :amount, :total_excluding_vat
32: 
33:     self.allowed_ransackable_attributes = ["memo"]
34: 
35:     def total_excluding_vat
36:       return_items.sum(&:total_excluding_vat)
37:     end
38: 
39:     def amount
40:       return_items.sum(:amount)
41:     end
42: 
43:     def currency
44:       order.currency
45:     end
46: 
47:     def refundable_amount
48:       order.item_total_before_tax + order.promo_total
49:     end
50: 
51:     def customer_returned_items?
52:       customer_returns.exists?
53:     end
54: 
55:     def can_cancel_return_items?
56:       return_items.any?(&:can_cancel?) || return_items.blank?
57:     end
58: 
59:     private
60: 
61:     def must_have_shipped_units
62:       if order.nil? || order.inventory_units.shipped.none?
63:         errors.add(:order, I18n.t("spree.has_no_shipped_units"))
64:       end
65:     end
66: 
67:     def generate_number
68:       self.number ||= loop do
69:         random = "RA#{Array.new(9) { rand(9) }.join}"
70:         break random unless self.class.exists?(number: random)
71:       end
72:     end
73: 
74:     def no_previously_exchanged_inventory_units
75:       if return_items.map(&:inventory_unit).any?(&:exchange_requested?)
76:         errors.add(:base, I18n.t("spree.return_items_cannot_be_created_for_inventory_units_that_are_already_awaiting_exchange"))
77:       end
78:     end
79: 
80:     def cancel_return_items
81:       return_items.each { |item| item.cancel! if item.can_cancel? }
82:     end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   class Reimbursement < Spree::Base
5:     class IncompleteReimbursementError < StandardError; end
6: 
7:     belongs_to :order, inverse_of: :reimbursements, optional: true
8:     belongs_to :customer_return, inverse_of: :reimbursements, touch: true, optional: true
9: 
10:     has_many :refunds, inverse_of: :reimbursement
11:     has_many :credits, inverse_of: :reimbursement, class_name: "Spree::Reimbursement::Credit"
12: 
13:     has_many :return_items, inverse_of: :reimbursement
14: 
15:     validates :order, presence: true
16:     validate :validate_return_items_belong_to_same_order
17: 
18:     accepts_nested_attributes_for :return_items, allow_destroy: true
19: 
20:     before_create :generate_number
21:     before_create :calculate_total
22: 
23:     scope :reimbursed, -> { where(reimbursement_status: "reimbursed") }
24: 
25:     # The reimbursement_tax_calculator property should be set to an object that responds to "call"
26:     # and accepts a reimbursement object. Invoking "call" should update the tax fields on the
27:     # associated ReturnItems.
28:     # This allows a store to easily integrate with third party tax services.
29:     class_attribute :reimbursement_tax_calculator
30:     self.reimbursement_tax_calculator = ReimbursementTaxCalculator
31:     # A separate attribute here allows you to use a more performant calculator for estimates
32:     # and a different one (e.g. one that hits a 3rd party API) for the final caluclations.
33:     class_attribute :reimbursement_simulator_tax_calculator
34:     self.reimbursement_simulator_tax_calculator = ReimbursementTaxCalculator
35: 
36:     # The reimbursement_models property should contain an array of all models that provide
37:     # reimbursements.
38:     # This allows a store to incorporate custom reimbursement methods that Spree doesn't know about.
39:     # Each model must implement a "total_amount_reimbursed_for" method.
40:     # Example:
41:     #   Refund.total_amount_reimbursed_for(reimbursement)
42:     # See the `reimbursement_generator` property regarding the generation of custom reimbursements.
43:     class_attribute :reimbursement_models
44:     self.reimbursement_models = [Spree::Refund, Spree::Reimbursement::Credit]
45: 
46:     # The reimbursement_performer property should be set to an object that responds to the following methods:
47:     # - #perform
48:     # - #simulate
49:     # see ReimbursementPerformer for details.
50:     # This allows a store to customize their reimbursement methods and logic.
51:     class_attribute :reimbursement_performer
52:     self.reimbursement_performer = ReimbursementPerformer
53: 
54:     include ::Spree::Config.state_machines.reimbursement
55: 
56:     class << self
57:       def build_from_customer_return(customer_return)
58:         order = customer_return.order
59:         order.reimbursements.build({
60:           customer_return:,
61:           return_items: customer_return.return_items.accepted.not_reimbursed
62:         })
63:       end
64:     end
65: 
66:     def display_total
67:       Spree::Money.new(total, {currency: order.currency})
68:     end
69: 
70:     def calculated_total
71:       # rounding down to handle edge cases for consecutive partial returns where rounding
72:       # might cause us to try to reimburse more than was originally billed
73:       return_items.to_a.sum(&:total).to_d.round(2, :down)
74:     end
75: 
76:     def paid_amount
77:       reimbursement_models.sum do |model|
78:         model.total_amount_reimbursed_for(self)
79:       end
80:     end
81: 
82:     def unpaid_amount
83:       total - paid_amount
84:     end
85: 
86:     def perform!(created_by:)
87:       reimbursement_tax_calculator.call(self)
88:       reload
89:       update!(total: calculated_total)
90: 
91:       reimbursement_performer.perform(self, created_by:)
92: 
93:       if unpaid_amount_within_tolerance?
94:         reimbursed!
95:         Spree::Bus.publish :reimbursement_reimbursed, reimbursement: self
96:       else
97:         errored!
98:         Spree::Bus.publish :reimbursement_errored, reimbursement: self
99:       end
100: 
101:       if errored?
102:         raise IncompleteReimbursementError, I18n.t("spree.validation.unpaid_amount_not_zero", amount: unpaid_amount)
103:       end
104:     end
105: 
106:     def simulate(created_by:)
107:       reimbursement_simulator_tax_calculator.call(self)
108:       reload
109:       update!(total: calculated_total)
110: 
111:       reimbursement_performer.simulate(self, created_by:)
112:     end
113: 
114:     def return_items_requiring_exchange
115:       return_items.select(&:exchange_required?)
116:     end
117: 
118:     def any_exchanges?
119:       return_items.any?(&:exchange_processed?)
120:     end
121: 
122:     def all_exchanges?
123:       return_items.all?(&:exchange_processed?)
124:     end
125: 
126:     # Accepts all return items, saves the reimbursement, and performs the reimbursement
127:     #
128:     # @api public
129:     # @param [Spree.user_class] created_by the user that is performing this action
130:     # @return [void]
131:     def return_all(created_by:)
132:       return_items.each(&:accept!)
133:       save!
134:       perform!(created_by:)
135:     end
136: 
137:     # The returned category is used as the category
138:     # for Spree::Reimbursement::Credit.default_creditable_class.
139:     #
140:     # @return [Spree::StoreCreditCategory]
141:     def store_credit_category
142:       Spree::StoreCreditCategory.find_by(name: Spree::StoreCreditCategory::REIMBURSEMENT)
143:     end
144: 
145:     private
146: 
147:     def calculate_total
148:       self.total ||= calculated_total
149:     end
150: 
151:     def generate_number
152:       self.number ||= loop do
153:         random = "RI#{Array.new(9) { rand(9) }.join}"
154:         break random unless self.class.exists?(number: random)
155:       end
156:     end
157: 
158:     def validate_return_items_belong_to_same_order
159:       if return_items.any? { |ri| ri.inventory_unit.order_id != order_id }
160:         errors.add(:base, :return_items_order_id_does_not_match)
161:       end
162:     end
163: 
164:     # If there are multiple different reimbursement types for a single
165:     # reimbursement we open ourselves to a one-cent rounding error for every
166:     # type over the first one. This is due to how we round #unpaid_amount and
167:     # how each reimbursement type will round as well. Since at this point the
168:     # payments and credits have already been processed, we should allow the
169:     # reimbursement to show as 'reimbursed' and not 'errored'.
170:     def unpaid_amount_within_tolerance?
171:       reimbursement_count = reimbursement_models.count do |model|
172:         model.total_amount_reimbursed_for(self) > 0
173:       end
174:       leniency = if reimbursement_count > 0
175:         (reimbursement_count - 1) * BigDecimal("0.01")
176:       else
177:         0
178:       end
179:       unpaid_amount.abs.between?(0, leniency)
180:     end
181:   end
182: end

(End of file - total 182 lines)
</content>

1: # frozen_string_literal: true

3: module Spree
4:   class Exchange
5:     class UnableToCreateShipments < StandardError; end
6:     extend ActiveModel::Naming
7: 
8:     def initialize(order, reimbursement_objects)
9:       @order = order
10:       @reimbursement_objects = reimbursement_objects
11:     end
12: 
13:     def description
14:       @reimbursement_objects.map do |reimbursement_object|
15:         "#{reimbursement_object.variant.options_text} => #{reimbursement_object.exchange_variant.options_text}"
16:       end.join(" | ")
17:     end
18: 
19:     def display_amount
20:       Spree::Money.new @reimbursement_objects.sum(&:total)
21:     end
22: 
23:     def perform!
24:       begin
25:         shipments = Spree::Config.stock.coordinator_class.new(
26:           @order,
27:           inventory_units: @reimbursement_objects.map(&:build_exchange_inventory_unit)
28:         ).shipments
29:       rescue Spree::Order::InsufficientStock
30:         raise UnableToCreateShipments.new("Could not generate shipments for all items. Out of stock?")
31:       end
32:       @order.shipments += shipments
33:       @order.save!
34:       shipments.each do |shipment|
35:         shipment.update_state
36:         shipment.finalize!
37:       end
38:     end
39: 
40:     def to_key
41:       nil
42:     end
43: 
44:     def self.param_key
45:       "spree_exchange"
46:     end
47:   end
48: end

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   module Stock
5:     # A simple implementation of Stock Coordination
6:     #
7:     # The algorithm for allocating inventory is naive:
8:     #   * For each available Stock Location
9:     #     * Allocate as much on hand inventory as possible from this location
10:     #     * Remove the amount allocated from the amount desired
11:     #   * Repeat but for backordered inventory
12:     #   * Combine allocated and on hand inventory into a single shipment per-location
13:     #
14:     # Allocation logic can be changed using a custom class (as
15:     # configured in Spree::Config::stock_allocator_class )
16:     #
17:     # After allocation, splitters are run on each Package (as configured in
18:     # Spree::Config.environment.stock_splitters)
19:     #
20:     # Finally, shipping rates are calculated using the class configured as
21:     # Spree::Config.stock.estimator_class.
22:     class SimpleCoordinator
23:       attr_reader :order
24: 
25:       # @api private
26:       attr_reader :inventory_units, :splitters, :stock_locations,
27:         :filtered_stock_locations, :inventory_units_by_variant, :desired,
28:         :availability, :allocator, :packages
29: 
30:       def initialize(order, inventory_units_deprecated = nil, inventory_units: nil)
31:         if inventory_units_deprecated
32:           Spree.deprecator.warn "Using the `inventory_units` positional " \
33:             "argument is deprecated in favor of using the keyword argument. "
34: 
35:           inventory_units ||= inventory_units_deprecated
36:         end
37: 
38:         @order = order
39:         @inventory_units =
40:           inventory_units || Spree::Config.stock.inventory_unit_builder_class.new(order).units
41:         @splitters = Spree::Config.environment.stock_splitters
42: 
43:         @filtered_stock_locations = Spree::Config.stock.location_filter_class.new(load_stock_locations, order).filter
44:         sorted_stock_locations = Spree::Config.stock.location_sorter_class.new(filtered_stock_locations).sort
45:         @stock_locations = sorted_stock_locations
46: 
47:         @inventory_units_by_variant = @inventory_units.group_by(&:variant)
48:         @desired = Spree::StockQuantities.new(inventory_units_by_variant.transform_values(&:count))
49:         @availability = Spree::Stock::Availability.new(
50:           variants: desired.variants,
51:           stock_locations:
52:         )
53: 
54:         @allocator = Spree::Config.stock.allocator_class.new(availability)
55:       end
56: 
57:       def shipments
58:         @shipments ||= begin
59:           @packages = build_packages
60:           shipments = build_shipments
61: 
62:           # Make sure we don't add the proposed shipments to the order
63:           order.shipments = order.shipments - shipments
64: 
65:           shipments
66:         end
67:       end
68: 
69:       private
70: 
71:       def load_stock_locations
72:         Spree::StockLocation.all
73:       end
74: 
75:       def build_shipments
76:         # Turn the Stock::Packages into a Shipment with rates
77:         packages.map do |package|
78:           shipment = package.shipment = package.to_shipment
79:           shipment.shipping_rates = Spree::Config.stock.estimator_class.new.shipping_rates(package)
80:           shipment
81:         end
82:       end
83: 
84:       def build_packages
85:         # Allocate any available on hand inventory and remaining desired inventory from backorders
86:         on_hand_packages, backordered_packages, leftover = allocator.allocate_inventory(desired)
87: 
88:         raise Spree::Order::InsufficientStock.new(items: leftover.quantities) unless leftover.empty?
89: 
90:         packages = stock_locations.map do |stock_location|
91:           # Combine on_hand and backorders into a single package per-location
92:           on_hand = on_hand_packages[stock_location.id] || Spree::StockQuantities.new
93:           backordered = backordered_packages[stock_location.id] || Spree::StockQuantities.new
94: 
95:           # Skip this location it has no inventory
96:           next if on_hand.empty? && backordered.empty?
97: 
98:           # Turn our raw quantities into a Stock::Package
99:           package = Spree::Stock::Package.new(stock_location)
100:           package.add_multiple(get_units(on_hand), :on_hand)
101:           package.add_multiple(get_units(backordered), :backordered)
102: 
103:           package
104:         end.compact
105: 
106:         # Split the packages
107:         split_packages(packages)
108:       end
109: 
110:       def split_packages(initial_packages)
111:         initial_packages.flat_map do |initial_package|
112:           stock_location = initial_package.stock_location
113:           Spree::Stock::SplitterChain.new(stock_location, @splitters).split([initial_package])
114:         end
115:       end
116: 
117:       def get_units(quantities)
118:         # Change our raw quantities back into inventory units
119:         quantities.flat_map do |variant, quantity|
120:           inventory_units_by_variant[variant].shift(quantity)
121:         end
122:       end
123:     end
124:   end
125: end

(End of file - total 125 lines)
</content>

<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   module Stock
5:     class AvailabilityValidator < ActiveModel::Validator
6:       def validate(line_item)
7:         if is_valid?(line_item)
8:           true
9:         else
10:           variant = line_item.variant
11:           display_name = variant.name.to_s
12:           display_name += %{ (#{variant.options_text})} unless variant.options_text.blank?
13: 
14:           line_item.errors.add(:quantity, I18n.t(
15:             "spree.selected_quantity_not_available",
16:             item: display_name.inspect
17:           ))
18:           false
19:         end
20:       end
21: 
22:       private
23: 
24:       def is_valid?(line_item)
25:         if line_item.inventory_units.empty?
26:           Spree::Config.stock.quantifier_class.new(line_item.variant).can_supply?(line_item.quantity)
27:         else
28:           quantity_by_stock_location_id = line_item.inventory_units.pending.joins(:shipment).group(:stock_location_id).count
29:           quantity_by_stock_location_id.all? do |stock_location_id, quantity|

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   module Stock
5:     class Package
6:       attr_reader :stock_location, :contents
7:       attr_accessor :shipment
8: 
9:       # @param stock_location [Spree::StockLocation] the stock location this package originates from
10:       # @param contents [Array<Spree::Stock::ContentItem>] the contents of this package
11:       def initialize(stock_location, contents = [])
12:         @stock_location = stock_location
13:         @contents = contents
14:       end
15: 
16:       # Adds an inventory unit to this package.
17:       #
18:       # @param inventory_unit [Spree::InventoryUnit] an inventory unit to be
19:       #   added to this package
20:       # @param state [:on_hand, :backordered] the state of the item to be
21:       #   added to this package
22:       def add(inventory_unit, state = :on_hand)
23:         contents << ContentItem.new(inventory_unit, state) unless find_item(inventory_unit)
24:       end
25: 
26:       # Adds multiple inventory units to this package.
27:       #
28:       # @param inventory_units [Array<Spree::InventoryUnit>] a collection of
29:       #   inventory units to be added to this package
30:       # @param state [:on_hand, :backordered] the state of the items to be
31:       #   added to this package
32:       def add_multiple(inventory_units, state = :on_hand)
33:         inventory_units.each { |inventory_unit| add(inventory_unit, state) }
34:       end
35: 
36:       # Removes a given inventory unit from this package.
37:       #
38:       # @param inventory_unit [Spree::InventoryUnit] the inventory unit to be
39:       #   removed from this package
40:       def remove(inventory_unit)
41:         item = find_item(inventory_unit)
42:         @contents -= [item] if item
43:       end
44: 
45:       # @return [Spree::Order] the order associated with this package
46:       def order
47:         # Fix regression that removed package.order.
48:         # Find it dynamically through an inventory_unit.
49:         contents.detect { |item| !!item.try(:line_item).try(:order) }.try(:line_item).try(:order)
50:       end
51: 
52:       # @return [Float] the summed weight of the contents of this package
53:       def weight
54:         contents.sum(&:weight)
55:       end
56: 
57:       # @return [Array<Spree::Stock::ContentItem>] the content items in this
58:       #   package which are on hand
59:       def on_hand
60:         contents.select(&:on_hand?)
61:       end
62: 
63:       # @return [Array<Spree::Stock::ContentItem>] the content items in this
64:       #   package which are backordered
65:       def backordered
66:         contents.select(&:backordered?)
67:       end
68: 
69:       # Find a content item in this package by inventory unit and optionally
70:       # state.
71:       #
72:       # @param inventory_unit [Spree::InventoryUnit] the desired inventory
73:       #   unit
74:       # @param state [:backordered, :on_hand, nil] the state of the desired
75:       #   content item, or nil for any state
76:       def find_item(inventory_unit, state = nil)
77:         contents.detect do |item|
78:           item.inventory_unit == inventory_unit &&
79:             (!state || item.state.to_s == state.to_s)
80:         end
81:       end
82: 
83:       # @param state [:backordered, :on_hand, nil] the state of the content
84:       #   items of which we want the quantity, or nil for the full quantity
85:       # @return [Fixnum] the number of inventory units in the package,
86:       #   counting only those in the given state if it was specified
87:       def quantity(state = nil)
88:         matched_contents = state.nil? ? contents : contents.select { |content| content.state.to_s == state.to_s }
89:         matched_contents.sum(&:quantity)
90:       end
91: 
92:       # @return [Boolean] true if there are no inventory units in this
93:       #   package
94:       def empty?
95:         quantity == 0
96:       end
97: 
98:       # @return [String] the currency of the order this package belongs to
99:       def currency
100:         order.currency
101:       end
102: 
103:       # @return [Array<Spree::ShippingCategory>] the shipping categories of the
104:       #   variants in this package
105:       def shipping_categories
106:         Spree::ShippingCategory.where(id: shipping_category_ids)
107:       end
108: 
109:       # @return [ActiveRecord::Relation] the [Spree::ShippingMethod]s available
110:       #   for this pacakge based on the stock location and shipping categories.
111:       def shipping_methods
112:         Spree::ShippingMethod
113:           .with_all_shipping_category_ids(shipping_category_ids)
114:           .available_in_stock_location(stock_location)
115:       end
116: 
117:       # @return [Spree::Shipment] a new shipment containing this package's
118:       #   inventory units, with the appropriate shipping rates and associated
119:       #   with the correct stock location
120:       def to_shipment
121:         # At this point we should only have one content item per inventory unit
122:         # across the entire set of inventory units to be shipped, which has
123:         # been taken care of by the Prioritizer
124:         contents.each { |content_item| content_item.inventory_unit.state = content_item.state.to_s }
125: 
126:         Spree::Shipment.new(
127:           order:,
128:           stock_location:,
129:           inventory_units: contents.map(&:inventory_unit)
130:         )
131:       end
132: 
133:       private
134: 
135:       # @return [Array<Fixnum>] the unique ids of all shipping categories of
136:       #   variants in this package
137:       def shipping_category_ids
138:         contents.map { |item| item.variant.shipping_category_id }.compact.uniq
139:       end
140:     end
141:   end
142: end

(End of file - total 142 lines)

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   module Stock
5:     class Estimator
6:       class ShipmentRequired < StandardError; end
7:       class OrderRequired < StandardError; end
8: 
9:       # Estimate the shipping rates for a package.
10:       #
11:       # @param package [Spree::Stock::Package] the package to be shipped
12:       # @param frontend_only [Boolean] restricts the shipping methods to only
13:       #   those marked frontend if truthy
14:       # @return [Array<Spree::ShippingRate>] the shipping rates sorted by
15:       #   descending cost, with the least costly marked "selected"
16:       def shipping_rates(package, frontend_only = true)
17:         raise ShipmentRequired if package.shipment.nil?
18:         raise OrderRequired if package.shipment.order.nil?
19: 
20:         rates = calculate_shipping_rates(package)
21:         rates.select! { |rate| rate.shipping_method.available_to_users? } if frontend_only
22:         choose_default_shipping_rate(rates)
23:         Spree::Config.shipping_rate_sorter_class.new(rates).sort
24:       end
25: 
26:       private
27: 
28:       def choose_default_shipping_rate(shipping_rates)
29:         unless shipping_rates.empty?
30:           default_shipping_rate = Spree::Config.shipping_rate_selector_class.new(shipping_rates).find_default
31:           default_shipping_rate.selected = true
32:         end
33:       end
34: 
35:       def calculate_shipping_rates(package)
36:         tax_calculator_class = Spree::Config.shipping_rate_tax_calculator_class
37:         tax_calculator = tax_calculator_class.new(package.shipment.order)
38:         shipping_methods(package).map do |shipping_method|
39:           cost = shipping_method.calculator.compute(package)
40:           if cost
41:             rate = shipping_method.shipping_rates.new(
42:               cost:,
43:               shipment: package.shipment
44:             )
45:             tax_calculator.calculate(rate).each do |tax|
46:               rate.taxes.new(
47:                 amount: tax.amount,
48:                 tax_rate: tax.tax_rate
49:               )
50:             end
51:             rate
52:           end
53:         end.compact
54:       end
55: 
56:       def shipping_methods(package)
57:         package.shipping_methods
58:           .available_to_store(package.shipment.order.store)
59:           .available_for_address(package.shipment.order.ship_address)
60:           .includes(:calculator)
61:           .to_a
62:           .select do |ship_method|
63:             calculator = ship_method.calculator
64:             calculator.available?(package) &&
65:               (calculator.preferences[:currency].blank? ||
66:                calculator.preferences[:currency] == package.shipment.order.currency)
67:         end
68:       end
69:     end
70:   end
71: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   module Stock
5:     # Service class to finalize inventory units, it means unstock the desired
6:     # quantity from related stock item and updates the given inventory units to
7:     # not be pending.
8:     class InventoryUnitsFinalizer
9:       # @attr_reader [Spree::InventoryUnit] inventory_units to be finalized
10:       attr_reader :inventory_units
11: 
12:       # @param [Spree::InventoryUnit] inventory_units to be finalized
13:       def initialize(inventory_units)
14:         @inventory_units = inventory_units
15:       end
16: 
17:       # Finalize the inventory units, unstock and mark them as not pending.
18:       def run!
19:         Spree::InventoryUnit.transaction do
20:           unstock_inventory_units
21:           finalize_inventory_units
22:         end
23:       end
24: 
25:       private
26: 
27:       def finalize_inventory_units
28:         inventory_units.map do |iu|
29:           iu.update_columns(
30:             pending: false,
31:             updated_at: Time.current
32:           )
33:         end
34:       end
35: 
36:       def unstock_inventory_units
37:         inventory_units.group_by(&:shipment).each do |shipment, units_by_shipment|
38:           units_by_shipment.group_by(&:variant).each do |variant, units|
39:             shipment.stock_location.unstock variant, units.count, shipment
40:           end
41:         end
42:       end
43:     end
44:   end
45: end

31:       end

7:     def update_cart(params)
8:       if order.update(params)
9:         unless order.completed?
10:           order.line_items = order.line_items.select { |li| li.quantity > 0 }
11:           # Update totals, then check if the order is eligible for any cart promotions.
12:           # If we do not update first, then the item total will be wrong and ItemTotal
13:           # promotion rules would not be triggered.
14:           reload_totals
15:           order.check_shipments_and_restart_checkout
16:           ::Spree::PromotionHandler::Cart.new(order).activate
17:         end
18:         reload_totals
19:         true
20:       else
21:         false
22:       end
23:     end
24: 
25:     private
26: 
27:     def after_add_or_remove(line_item, options = {})
28:       reload_totals
29:       shipment = options[:shipment]
30:       shipment.present? ? shipment.update_amounts : order.check_shipments_and_restart_checkout
31:       ::Spree::PromotionHandler::Cart.new(order, line_item).activate
32:       reload_totals
33:       line_item
34:     end
35:   end
36: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   module PromotionHandler
5:     # Decides which promotion should be activated given the current order context
6:     #
7:     # By activated it doesn't necessarily mean that the order will have a
8:     # discount for every activated promotion. It means that the discount will be
9:     # created and might eventually become eligible. The intention here is to
10:     # reduce overhead. e.g. a promotion that requires item A to be eligible
11:     # shouldn't be eligible unless item A is added to the order.
12:     #
13:     # It can be used as a wrapper for custom handlers as well. Different
14:     # applications might have completely different requirements to make
15:     # the promotions system accurate and performant. Here they can plug custom
16:     # handler to activate promos as they wish once an item is added to cart
17:     class Cart
18:       attr_reader :line_item, :order
19:       attr_accessor :error, :success
20: 
21:       def initialize(order, line_item = nil)
22:         @order, @line_item = order, line_item
23:       end
24: 
25:       def activate
26:         promotions.each do |promotion|
27:           if (line_item && promotion.eligible?(line_item, promotion_code: promotion_code(promotion))) || promotion.eligible?(order, promotion_code: promotion_code(promotion))
28:             promotion.activate(line_item:, order:, promotion_code: promotion_code(promotion))
29:           end
30:         end
31:       end
32: 
33:       private
34: 
35:       def promotions
36:         promos = connected_order_promotions | sale_promotions
37:         promos.flat_map(&:promotion_actions).group_by(&:preload_relations).each do |preload_relations, actions|
38:           preload(records: actions, associations: preload_relations)
39:         end
40:         promos.flat_map(&:promotion_rules).group_by(&:preload_relations).each do |preload_relations, rules|
41:           preload(records: rules, associations: preload_relations)
42:         end
43:         promos
44:       end
45: 
46:       def preload(records:, associations:)
47:         if Rails::VERSION::MAJOR >= 7
48:           ActiveRecord::Associations::Preloader.new(records:, associations:).call
49:         else
50:           ActiveRecord::Associations::Preloader.new.preload(records, associations)
51:         end
52:       end
53: 
54:       def connected_order_promotions
55:         order.promotions.active.includes(promotion_includes)
56:       end
57: 
58:       def sale_promotions
59:         Spree::Promotion.where(apply_automatically: true).active.includes(promotion_includes)
60:       end
61: 
62:       def promotion_code(promotion)
63:         order_promotion = order.order_promotions.detect { |op| op.promotion_id == promotion.id }
64:         order_promotion.presence&.promotion_code
65:       end
66: 
67:       def promotion_includes
68:         [
69:           :promotion_rules,
70:           :promotion_actions
71:         ]
72:       end
73:     end
74:   end
75: end

(End of file - total 75 lines)
</content>

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   module PromotionHandler
5:     class Coupon
6:       attr_reader :order, :coupon_code
7:       attr_accessor :error, :success, :status_code
8: 
9:       def initialize(order)
10:         @order = order
11:         @coupon_code = order.coupon_code&.downcase
12:       end
13: 
14:       def can_apply?
15:         Spree::Promotion.order_activatable?(order)
16:       end
17: 
18:       def apply
19:         if coupon_code.present?
20:           if promotion.present? && promotion.active? && promotion.actions.exists?
21:             handle_present_promotion(promotion)
22:           elsif promotion_code&.promotion&.expired?
23:             set_error_code :coupon_code_expired
24:           else
25:             set_error_code :coupon_code_not_found
26:           end
27:         end
28: 
29:         self
30:       end
31: 
32:       def remove
33:         if promotion.blank?
34:           set_error_code :coupon_code_not_found
35:         elsif !promotion_exists_on_order?(order, promotion)
36:           set_error_code :coupon_code_not_present
37:         else
38:           promotion.remove_from(order)
39:           order.recalculate
40:           set_success_code :coupon_code_removed
41:         end
42: 
43:         self
44:       end
45: 
46:       def set_success_code(status_code)
47:         @status_code = status_code
48:         @success = I18n.t(status_code, scope: "spree")
49:       end
50: 
51:       def set_error_code(status_code, options = {})
52:         @status_code = status_code
53:         @error = options[:error] || I18n.t(status_code, scope: "spree")
54:       end
55: 
56:       def promotion
57:         @promotion ||= if promotion_code&.promotion&.active?
58:           promotion_code.promotion
59:         end
60:       end
61: 
62:       def successful?
63:         success.present? && error.blank?
64:       end
65: 
66:       private
67: 
68:       def promotion_code
69:         @promotion_code ||= Spree::PromotionCode.where(value: coupon_code).first
70:       end
71: 
72:       def handle_present_promotion(promotion)
73:         return promotion_usage_limit_exceeded if promotion.usage_limit_exceeded? || promotion_code.usage_limit_exceeded?
74:         return promotion_applied if promotion_exists_on_order?(order, promotion)
75: 
76:         unless promotion.eligible?(order, promotion_code:)
77:           set_promotion_eligibility_error_code(promotion)
78:           return error || ineligible_for_this_order
79:         end
80: 
81:         # If any of the actions for the promotion return `true`,
82:         # then result here will also be `true`.
83:         result = promotion.activate(order:, promotion_code:)
84:         if result
85:           order.recalculate
86:           set_success_code :coupon_code_applied
87:         else
88:           set_error_code :coupon_code_unknown_error
89:         end
90:       end
91: 
92:       def set_promotion_eligibility_error_code(promotion)
93:         return unless eligibility_error_code_present?(promotion)
94: 
95:         eligibility_error = promotion.eligibility_errors.details[:base].first
96: 
97:         @status_code = eligibility_error[:error_code]
98:         @error = eligibility_error[:error]
99:       end
100: 
101:       def promotion_usage_limit_exceeded
102:         set_error_code :coupon_code_max_usage
103:       end
104: 
105:       def ineligible_for_this_order
106:         set_error_code :coupon_code_not_eligible
107:       end
108: 
109:       def promotion_applied
110:         set_error_code :coupon_code_already_applied
111:       end
112: 
113:       def promotion_exists_on_order?(order, promotion)
114:         order.promotions.include? promotion
115:       end
116: 
117:       def eligibility_error_code_present?(promotion)
118:         promotion.eligibility_errors.present? &&
119:           promotion.eligibility_errors.details.present? &&
120:           promotion.eligibility_errors.details.key?(:base) &&
121:           promotion.eligibility_errors.details[:base].first[:error_code].present?
122:       end
123:     end
124:   end
125: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   class Promotion < Spree::Base
5:     module Actions
6:       class CreateAdjustment < PromotionAction
7:         include Spree::CalculatedAdjustments
8:         include Spree::AdjustmentSource
9: 
10:         has_many :adjustments, as: :source, dependent: :restrict_with_error
11: 
12:         delegate :eligible?, to: :promotion
13: 
14:         before_validation :ensure_action_has_calculator
15:         before_destroy :remove_adjustments_from_incomplete_orders
16:         before_discard :remove_adjustments_from_incomplete_orders
17: 
18:         def preload_relations
19:           [:calculator]
20:         end
21: 
22:         # Creates the adjustment related to a promotion for the order passed
23:         # through options hash
24:         #
25:         # Returns `true` if an adjustment is applied to an order,
26:         # `false` if the promotion has already been applied.
27:         def perform(options = {})
28:           order = options[:order]
29:           return if promotion_credit_exists?(order)
30: 
31:           amount = compute_amount(order)
32:           order.adjustments.create!(
33:             amount:,
34:             order:,
35:             source: self,
36:             promotion_code: options[:promotion_code],
37:             label: I18n.t("spree.adjustment_labels.order", promotion: Spree::Promotion.model_name.human, promotion_name: promotion.name)
38:           )
39:           true
40:         end
41: 
42:         # Ensure a negative amount which does not exceed the sum of the order's
43:         # item_total and ship_total
44:         def compute_amount(calculable)
45:           amount = calculator.compute(calculable)
46:           amount ||= Spree::ZERO
47:           amount = amount.abs
48:           [(calculable.item_total + calculable.ship_total), amount].min * -1
49:         end
50: 
51:         # Removes any adjustments generated by this action from the order.
52:         # @param order [Spree::Order] the order to remove the action from.
53:         # @return [void]
54:         def remove_from(order)
55:           order.adjustments.each do |adjustment|
56:             if adjustment.source == self
57:               order.adjustments.destroy(adjustment)
58:             end
59:           end
60:         end
61: 
62:         private
63: 
64:         # Tells us if there if the specified promotion is already associated with the line item
65:         # regardless of whether or not its currently eligible. Useful because generally
66:         # you would only want a promotion action to apply to order no more than once.
67:         #
68:         # Receives an adjustment +source+ (here a PromotionAction object) and tells
69:         # if the order has adjustments from that already
70:         def promotion_credit_exists?(adjustable)
71:           adjustments.where(adjustable_id: adjustable.id).exists?
72:         end
73: 
74:         def ensure_action_has_calculator
75:           return if calculator
76:           self.calculator = Calculator::FlatPercentItemTotal.new
77:         end
78:       end
79:     end
80:   end
81: end

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module SolidusPromotions
4:   class OrderAdjuster
5:     class DiscountOrder
6:       attr_reader :order, :promotions, :dry_run
7: 
8:       def initialize(order, promotions, dry_run: false)
9:         if dry_run
10:           Spree.deprecator.warn <<~MSG
11:             Passing `dry_run` to `SolidusPromotions::OrderAdjuster::DiscountOrder` is deprecated.
12:             Use `Spree::Config.promotions.eligibility_checker_class.new(order: order, promotion: promotion).call` instead.
13:           MSG
14:         end
15:         @order = order
16:         @promotions = promotions
17:         @dry_run = dry_run
18:       end
19: 
20:       def call
21:         return order if order.shipped?
22: 
23:         SolidusPromotions::Promotion.ordered_lanes.each do |lane|
24:           SolidusPromotions::PromotionLane.set(current_lane: lane) do
25:             lane_promotions = promotions.select { |promotion| promotion.lane == lane }
26:             lane_benefits = eligible_benefits_for_promotable(lane_promotions.flat_map(&:benefits), order)
27:             perform_order_benefits(lane_benefits, lane) unless dry_run
28:             adjust_line_items(lane_benefits)
29:             adjust_shipments(lane_benefits)
30:             adjust_shipping_rates(lane_benefits)
31:           end
32:         end
33: 
34:         order
35:       end
36: 
37:       private
38: 
39:       def perform_order_benefits(lane_benefits, lane)
40:         lane_benefits.filter_map do |benefit|
41:           benefit.respond_to?(:perform) && benefit.perform(order)
42:         end
43: 
44:         order.line_items.filter_map do |line_item|
45:           line_item.managed_by_order_benefit &&
46:             line_item.managed_by_order_benefit.promotion.lane == lane &&
47:             !line_item.managed_by_order_benefit.in?(lane_benefits) &&
48:             line_item.managed_by_order_benefit.remove_from(order)
49:         end
50:       end
51: 
52:       def adjust_line_items(benefits)
53:         order.discountable_line_items.filter_map do |line_item|
54:           next unless line_item.variant.product.promotionable?
55:           generate_discounts(benefits, line_item)
56:         end
57:       end
58: 
59:       def adjust_shipments(benefits)
60:         order.shipments.map do |shipment|
61:           generate_discounts(benefits, shipment)
62:         end
63:       end
64: 
65:       def adjust_shipping_rates(benefits)
66:         order.shipments.flat_map(&:shipping_rates).filter_map do |rate|
67:           next unless rate.cost
68:           generate_discounts(benefits, rate)
69:         end
70:       end
71: 
72:       def eligible_benefits_for_promotable(possible_benefits, promotable)
73:         possible_benefits.select do |candidate|
74:           candidate.eligible_by_applicable_conditions?(promotable, dry_run: dry_run)
75:         end
76:       end
77: 
78:       def generate_discounts(possible_benefits, item)
79:         eligible_benefits = eligible_benefits_for_promotable(possible_benefits, item)
80:         discounts = eligible_benefits.filter_map do |benefit|
81:           benefit.can_discount?(item) && benefit.discount(item)
82:         end
83:         chosen_discounts = SolidusPromotions.config.discount_chooser_class.new(discounts).call
84:         (item.current_lane_discounts - chosen_discounts).each(&:mark_for_destruction)
85:       end
86:     end
87:   end
88: end

(End of file - total 88 lines)

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   module TaxCalculator
5:     # Default implementation for tax calculations. Will go through all line
6:     # items and shipments and calculate their tax based on tax rates in the DB.
7:     #
8:     # The class used for tax calculation is configurable, so that the
9:     # calculation can easily be pushed to third-party services. Users looking
10:     # to provide their own calculator should adhere to the API of this class.
11:     class Default
12:       include Spree::Tax::TaxHelpers
13: 
14:       # Create a new tax calculator.
15:       #
16:       # @param [Spree::Order] order the order to calculator taxes on
17:       # @return [Spree::TaxCalculator::Default] a Spree::TaxCalculator::Default object
18:       def initialize(order)
19:         @order = order
20:       end
21: 
22:       # Calculate taxes for an order.
23:       #
24:       # @return [Spree::Tax::OrderTax] the calculated taxes for the order
25:       def calculate
26:         Spree::Tax::OrderTax.new(
27:           order_id: order.id,
28:           order_taxes: order_rates,
29:           line_item_taxes: line_item_rates,
30:           shipment_taxes: shipment_rates
31:         )
32:       end
33: 
34:       private
35: 
36:       attr_reader :order
37: 
38:       # Calculate the order-level taxes.
39:       #
40:       # @private
41:       # @return [Array<Spree::Tax::ItemTax>] calculated taxes for the order
42:       def order_rates
43:         rates_for_order.map do |rate|
44:           amount = rate.compute_amount(order)
45: 
46:           Spree::Tax::ItemTax.new(
47:             label: rate.adjustment_label(amount),
48:             tax_rate: rate,
49:             amount:,
50:             included_in_price: rate.included_in_price
51:           )
52:         end
53:       end
54: 
55:       # Calculate the taxes for line items.
56:       #
57:       # @private
58:       # @return [Array<Spree::Tax::ItemTax>] calculated taxes for the line items
59:       def line_item_rates
60:         order.line_items.flat_map do |line_item|
61:           calculate_rates(line_item)
62:         end
63:       end
64: 
65:       # Calculate the taxes for shipments.
66:       #
67:       # @private
68:       # @return [Array<Spree::Tax::ItemTax>] calculated taxes for the shipments
69:       def shipment_rates
70:         order.shipments.flat_map do |shipment|
71:           calculate_rates(shipment)
72:         end
73:       end
74: 
75:       # Calculate the taxes for a single item.
76:       #
77:       # The item could be either a {Spree::LineItem} or a {Spree::Shipment}.
78:       #
79:       # Will go through all applicable rates for an item and create a new
80:       # {Spree::Tax::ItemTax} containing the calculated taxes for the item.
81:       #
82:       # @private
83:       # @return [Array<Spree::Tax::ItemTax>] calculated taxes for the item
84:       def calculate_rates(item)
85:         rates_for_item(item).map do |rate|
86:           amount = rate.compute_amount(item)
87: 
88:           Spree::Tax::ItemTax.new(
89:             item_id: item.id,
90:             label: rate.adjustment_label(amount),
91:             tax_rate: rate,
92:             amount:,
93:             included_in_price: rate.included_in_price
94:           )
95:         end
96:       end
97: 
98:       # @private
99:       # @return [Array<Spree::TaxRate>] rates that apply to an order
100:       def rates_for_order
101:         tax_category_ids = Set[
102:           *@order.line_items.map(&:variant_tax_category_id),
103:           *@order.shipments.map(&:tax_category_id)
104:         ]
105:         rates = Spree::TaxRate.active.order_level.for_address(@order.tax_address)
106:         rates.select do |rate|
107:           tax_category_ids.intersect?(rate.tax_category_ids.to_set)
108:         end
109:       end
110:     end
111:   end
112: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true

3: class Spree::ItemTotal
4:   def initialize(item)
5:     @item = item
6:   end
7: 
8:   def recalculate!
9:     tax_adjustments = item.adjustments.select do |adjustment|
10:       adjustment.tax? && !adjustment.marked_for_destruction?
11:     end
12: 
13:     # Included tax adjustments are those which are included in the price.
14:     # These ones should not affect the eventual total price.
15:     #
16:     # Additional tax adjustments are the opposite, affecting the final total.
17:     item.included_tax_total = tax_adjustments.select(&:included?).sum(&:amount)
18:     item.additional_tax_total = tax_adjustments.reject(&:included?).sum(&:amount)
19: 
20:     item.adjustment_total = item.adjustments.reject { |adjustment|
21:       adjustment.marked_for_destruction? || adjustment.included?
22:     }.sum(&:amount)
23:   end
24: 
25:   private
26: 
27:   attr_reader :item
28: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   class Payment < Spree::Base
5:     module Processing
6:       # "process!" means:
7:       #   - Do nothing when:
8:       #     - There is no payment method
9:       #     - The payment method does not require a source
10:       #     - The payment is in the "processing" state
11:       #     - 'auto_capture?' is false and the payment is already authorized.
12:       #   - Raise an exception when:
13:       #     - The source is missing or invalid
14:       #     - The payment is in a state that cannot transition to 'processing'
15:       #       (failed/void/invalid states). Note: 'completed' can transition to
16:       #       'processing' and thus calling #process! on a completed Payment
17:       #       will attempt to re-authorize/re-purchase the payment.
18:       #   - Otherwise:
19:       #     - If 'auto_capture?' is true:
20:       #       - Call #purchase on the payment gateway. (i.e. authorize+capture)
21:       #         even if the payment is already completed.
22:       #     - Else:
23:       #       - Call #authorize on the payment gateway even if the payment is
24:       #         already completed.
25:       def process!
26:         return if payment_method.nil?
27: 
28:         if payment_method.auto_capture?
29:           purchase!
30:         elsif pending?
31:           # do nothing. already authorized.
32:         else
33:           authorize!
34:         end
35:       end
36: 
37:       def authorize!
38:         return unless check_payment_preconditions!
39: 
40:         started_processing!
41: 
42:         protect_from_connection_error do
43:           response = payment_method.authorize(
44:             money.money.cents,
45:             source,
46:             gateway_options
47:           )
48:           pend! if handle_response(response)
49:         end
50:       end
51: 
52:       # Captures the entire amount of a payment.
53:       def purchase!
54:         return unless check_payment_preconditions!
55: 
56:         started_processing!
57: 
58:         protect_from_connection_error do
59:           response = payment_method.purchase(
60:             money.money.cents,
61:             source,
62:             gateway_options
63:           )
64:           complete! if handle_response(response)
65:         end
66: 
67:         capture_events.create!(amount:)
68:       end
69: 
70:       # Takes the amount in cents to capture.
71:       # Can be used to capture partial amounts of a payment, and will create
72:       # a new pending payment record for the remaining amount to capture later.
73:       def capture!(capture_amount = nil)
74:         return true if completed?
75:         return false unless amount.positive?
76: 
77:         capture_amount ||= money.money.cents
78:         started_processing!
79:         protect_from_connection_error do
80:           # Standard ActiveMerchant capture usage
81:           response = payment_method.capture(
82:             capture_amount,
83:             response_code,
84:             gateway_options
85:           )
86:           money = ::Money.new(capture_amount, currency)
87:           capture_events.create!(amount: money.to_d)
88:           update!(amount: captured_amount)
89:           complete! if handle_response(response)
90:         end
91:       end
92: 
93:       def void_transaction!
94:         return true if void?
95:         return false unless amount.positive?
96: 
97:         protect_from_connection_error do
98:           response = if payment_method.payment_profiles_supported?
99:             # Gateways supporting payment profiles will need access to credit card object because this stores the payment profile information
100:             # so supply the authorization itself as well as the credit card, rather than just the authorization code
101:             payment_method.void(response_code, source, gateway_options)
102:           else
103:             # Standard ActiveMerchant void usage
104:             payment_method.void(response_code, gateway_options)
105:           end
106: 
107:           handle_void_response(response)
108:         end
109:       end
110: 
111:       def cancel!
112:         Spree::Config.payment_canceller.cancel(self)
113:       end
114: 
115:       def gateway_options
116:         order.reload
117:         options = {
118:           email: order.email,
119:           customer: order.email,
120:           customer_id: order.user_id,
121:           ip: order.last_ip_address,
122:           # Need to pass in a unique identifier here to make some
123:           # payment gateways happy.
124:           #
125:           # For more information, please see Spree::Payment#set_unique_identifier
126:           order_id: gateway_order_id,
127:           # The originator is passed to options used by the payment method.
128:           # One example of a place that it is used is in:
129:           # app/models/spree/payment_method/store_credit.rb
130:           originator: self
131:         }
132: 
133:         options[:shipping] = order.ship_total * 100
134:         options[:tax] = order.additional_tax_total * 100
135:         options[:subtotal] = order.item_total * 100
136:         options[:discount] = order.promo_total * 100
137:         options[:currency] = currency
138: 
139:         bill_address = source.try(:address)
140:         bill_address ||= order.bill_address
141: 
142:         options[:billing_address] = bill_address.try!(:active_merchant_hash)
143:         options[:shipping_address] = order.ship_address.try!(:active_merchant_hash)
144: 
145:         options
146:       end
147: 
148:       # The unique identifier to be passed in to the payment gateway
149:       def gateway_order_id
150:         "#{order.number}-#{number}"
151:       end
152: 
153:       def handle_void_response(response)
154:         record_response(response)
155: 
156:         if response.success?
157:           self.response_code = response.authorization
158:           void
159:         else
160:           gateway_error(response)
161:         end
162:       end
163: 
164:       private
165: 
166:       # @raises Spree::Core::GatewayError
167:       def check_payment_preconditions!
168:         return if processing?
169:         return unless payment_method
170:         return unless payment_method.source_required?
171: 
172:         unless source
173:           gateway_error(I18n.t("spree.payment_processing_failed"))
174:         end
175: 
176:         unless payment_method.supports?(source)
177:           invalidate!
178:           gateway_error(I18n.t("spree.payment_method_not_supported"))
179:         end
180: 
181:         true
182:       end
183: 
184:       # @returns true if the response is successful
185:       # @returns false (and calls #failure) if the response is not successful
186:       def handle_response(response)
187:         record_response(response)
188: 
189:         unless response.success?
190:           failure
191:           gateway_error(response)
192:           return false
193:         end
194: 
195:         unless response.authorization.nil?
196:           self.response_code = response.authorization
197:           self.avs_response = response.avs_result["code"]
198: 
199:           if response.cvv_result
200:             self.cvv_response_code = response.cvv_result["code"]
201:             self.cvv_response_message = response.cvv_result["message"]
202:           end
203:         end
204: 
205:         true
206:       end
207: 
208:       def record_response(response)
209:         log_entries.create!(parsed_payment_response_details_with_fallback: response)
210:       end
211: 
212:       def protect_from_connection_error
213:         yield
214:       rescue ActiveMerchant::ConnectionError => error
215:         gateway_error(error)
216:       end
217: 
218:       def gateway_error(error)
219:         message, log = case error
220:         when ActiveMerchant::Billing::Response
221:           [
222:             error.params["message"] || error.params["response_reason_text"] || error.message,
223:             basic_response_info(error)
224:           ]
225:         when ActiveMerchant::ConnectionError
226:           [I18n.t("spree.unable_to_connect_to_gateway")] * 2
227:         else
228:           [error.to_s, error]
229:         end
230: 
231:         logger.error("#{I18n.t("spree.gateway_error")}: #{log}")
232:         raise Core::GatewayError.new(message)
233:       end
234: 
235:       # The gateway response information without the params since the params
236:       # can contain PII.
237:       def basic_response_info(response)
238:         {
239:           message: response.message,
240:           test: response.test,
241:           authorization: response.authorization,
242:           avs_result: response.avs_result,
243:           cvv_result: response.cvv_result,
244:           error_code: response.error_code,
245:           emv_authorization: response.emv_authorization,
246:           gateway_order_id:,
247:           order_number: order.number
248:         }
249:       end
250:     end
251:   end
252: end

(End of file - total 252 lines)

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   class Refund < Spree::Base
5:     include Metadata
6: 
7:     belongs_to :payment, inverse_of: :refunds, optional: true
8:     belongs_to :reason, class_name: "Spree::RefundReason", foreign_key: :refund_reason_id, optional: true
9:     belongs_to :reimbursement, inverse_of: :refunds, optional: true
10: 
11:     has_many :log_entries, as: :source
12: 
13:     validates :payment, presence: true
14:     validates :reason, presence: true
15:     validates :amount, presence: true, numericality: {greater_than: 0}
16: 
17:     validate :amount_is_less_than_or_equal_to_allowed_amount, on: :create
18: 
19:     attr_reader :perform_response
20: 
21:     scope :non_reimbursement, -> { where(reimbursement_id: nil) }
22: 
23:     delegate :currency, to: :payment
24: 
25:     def money
26:       Spree::Money.new(amount, {currency:})
27:     end
28:     alias_method :display_amount, :money
29: 
30:     class << self
31:       def total_amount_reimbursed_for(reimbursement)
32:         reimbursement.refunds.to_a.sum(&:amount)
33:       end
34:     end
35: 
36:     # Sets this price's amount to a new value, parsing it if the new value is
37:     # a string.
38:     #
39:     # @param price [String, #to_d] a new amount
40:     def amount=(price)
41:       self[:amount] = Spree::LocalizedNumber.parse(price)
42:     end
43: 
44:     def description
45:       payment.payment_method.name
46:     end
47: 
48:     # Must be called for the refund transaction to be processed.
49:     #
50:     # Attempts to perform the refund,
51:     # raises an error if the refund fails.
52:     def perform!
53:       return true if transaction_id.present?
54: 
55:       credit_cents = money.cents
56: 
57:       @perform_response = process!(credit_cents)
58:       log_entries.build(parsed_payment_response_details_with_fallback: perform_response)
59: 
60:       self.transaction_id = perform_response.authorization
61:       save!
62: 
63:       update_order
64:     end
65: 
66:     private
67: 
68:     # return an activemerchant response object if successful or else raise an error
69:     def process!(credit_cents)
70:       response = if payment.payment_method.payment_profiles_supported?
71:         payment.payment_method.credit(credit_cents, payment.source, payment.transaction_id, {originator: self})
72:       else
73:         payment.payment_method.credit(credit_cents, payment.transaction_id, {originator: self})
74:       end
75: 
76:       if !response.success?
77:         logger.error(I18n.t("spree.gateway_error") + "  #{response.to_yaml}")
78:         text = response.params["message"] || response.params["response_reason_text"] || response.message
79:         raise Core::GatewayError.new(text)
80:       end
81: 
82:       response
83:     rescue ActiveMerchant::ConnectionError => error
84:       logger.error(I18n.t("spree.gateway_error") + "  #{error.inspect}")
85:       raise Core::GatewayError.new(I18n.t("spree.unable_to_connect_to_gateway"))
86:     end
87: 
88:     def amount_is_less_than_or_equal_to_allowed_amount
89:       if payment && amount > payment.credit_allowed
90:         errors.add(:amount, :greater_than_allowed)
91:       end
92:     end
93: 
94:     def update_order
95:       payment.order.recalculate
96:     end
97:   end
98: end
