1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: # This represents an inventory unit that has been canceled from an order after it has already been completed
4: # The reason specifies why it was canceled.
5: # This class should encapsulate logic related to canceling inventory after order complete
6: class Spree::UnitCancel < Spree::Base
7:   SHORT_SHIP = "Short Ship"
8:   DEFAULT_REASON = "Cancel"
9: 
10:   belongs_to :inventory_unit, optional: true
11:   has_one :adjustment, as: :source, dependent: :destroy
12: 
13:   validates :inventory_unit, presence: true
14: 
15:   # Creates necessary cancel adjustments for the line item.
16:   def adjust!
17:     raise "Adjustment is already created" if adjustment
18: 
19:     amount = compute_amount(inventory_unit.line_item)
20: 
21:     self.adjustment = inventory_unit.line_item.adjustments.create!(
22:       source: self,
23:       amount:,
24:       order: inventory_unit.order,
25:       label: "#{I18n.t("spree.cancellation")} - #{reason}",
26:       finalized: true
27:     )
28: 
29:     inventory_unit.line_item.order.recalculate
30:     adjustment
31:   end
32: 
33:   # This method is used by Adjustment#update to recalculate the cost.
34:   def compute_amount(line_item)
35:     raise "Adjustable does not match line item" unless line_item == inventory_unit.line_item
36: 
37:     -weighted_line_item_amount(line_item)
38:   end
39: 
40:   private
41: 
42:   def weighted_line_item_amount(line_item)
43:     quantity_of_line_item = quantity_of_line_item(line_item)
44:     raise ZeroDivisionError, "Line Item does not have any inventory units available to cancel" if quantity_of_line_item.zero?
45: 
46:     line_item.total_before_tax / quantity_of_line_item
47:   end
48: 
49:   def quantity_of_line_item(line_item)
50:     BigDecimal(line_item.inventory_units.not_canceled.count { |element| !element.original_return_item })
51:   end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   class CustomerReturn < Spree::Base
5:     include Metadata
6: 
7:     belongs_to :stock_location
8: 
9:     has_many :return_items, inverse_of: :customer_return
10:     has_many :return_authorizations, through: :return_items
11:     has_many :reimbursements, inverse_of: :customer_return
12: 
13:     after_create :process_return!
14:     before_create :generate_number
15: 
16:     validates :return_items, presence: true
17:     validate :return_items_belong_to_same_order
18: 
19:     accepts_nested_attributes_for :return_items
20: 
21:     self.allowed_ransackable_attributes = ["number"]
22: 
23:     extend DisplayMoney
24: 
25:     money_methods :total, :total_excluding_vat, :amount
26: 
27:     delegate :currency, to: :order
28:     delegate :id, to: :order, prefix: true, allow_nil: true
29: 
30:     def total
31:       return_items.sum(&:total)
32:     end
33: 
34:     def total_excluding_vat
35:       return_items.sum(&:total_excluding_vat)
36:     end
37: 
38:     def amount
39:       return_items.sum(:amount)
40:     end
41: 
42:     # Temporarily tie a customer_return to one order
43:     def order
44:       return nil if return_items.blank?
45: 
46:       return_items.first.inventory_unit&.order
47:     end
48: 
49:     def fully_reimbursed?
50:       completely_decided? && return_items.accepted.includes(:reimbursement).all? { |return_item| return_item.reimbursement.try(:reimbursed?) }
51:     end
52: 
53:     def completely_decided?
54:       !return_items.undecided.exists?
55:     end
56: 
57:     def process_return!
58:       order.return! if order.can_return?
59:     end
60: 
61:     private
62: 
63:     def generate_number
64:       self.number ||= loop do
65:         random = "CR#{Array.new(9) { rand(9) }.join}"
66:         break random unless self.class.exists?(number: random)
67:       end
68:     end
69: 
70:     def return_items_belong_to_same_order
71:       if return_items.reject { |return_item| return_item.inventory_unit&.order_id == order_id }.any?
72:         errors.add(:base, I18n.t("spree.return_items_cannot_be_associated_with_multiple_orders"))
73:       end
74:     end
75: 
76:     def inventory_units
77:       return_items.flat_map(&:inventory_unit)
78:     end
79:   end
80: end

6:     include Omnes::Subscriber

9:       with: :send_inventory_cancellation_email,

12:     # Sends inventory cancellation email to the user.

18:       order = event[:order]
19:       inventory_units = event[:inventory_units]
20: 
21:       Spree::Config
22:         .order_mailer_class
23:         .inventory_cancellation_email(order, inventory_units.to_a)
24:         .deliver_later

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: # This class is responsible for building a default payment on an order, using a
4: # payment source that is already in the user's "wallet" and is marked
5: # as being the default payment source.
6: class Spree::Wallet::DefaultPaymentBuilder
7:   def initialize(order)
8:     @order = order
9:   end
10: 
11:   # Build a payment to be added to an order prior to moving into the "payment"
12:   # state.
13:   #
14:   # @return [Payment] the unsaved payment to be added, or nil if none.
15:   def build
16:     default = order.user.try!(:wallet).try!(:default_wallet_payment_source)
17:     if default && order.payments.where(source_type: default.payment_source_type).none?
18:       Spree::Payment.new(
19:         payment_method: default.payment_source.payment_method,
20:         source: default.payment_source
21:       )
22:     end
23:   end
24: 
25:   private
26: 
27:   attr_reader :order
28: end

<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   class ReturnItem < Spree::Base
5:     INTERMEDIATE_RECEPTION_STATUSES = %i[given_to_customer lost_in_transit shipped_wrong_item short_shipped in_transit]
6:     COMPLETED_RECEPTION_STATUSES = INTERMEDIATE_RECEPTION_STATUSES + [:received]
7: 
8:     # @!scope class
9:     # @!attribute return_eligibility_validator
10:     # Configurable validator for determining whether given return item is
11:     # eligible for return.
12:     # @return [Class]
13:     class_attribute :return_eligibility_validator
14:     self.return_eligibility_validator = ReturnItem::EligibilityValidator::Default
15: 
16:     # @!scope class
17:     # @!attribute exchange_variant_engine
18:     # Configurable engine for determining which variants can be exchanged for a
19:     # given variant.
20:     # @return [Class]
21:     class_attribute :exchange_variant_engine
22:     self.exchange_variant_engine = ReturnItem::ExchangeVariantEligibility::SameProduct
23: 
24:     # @!scope class
25:     # @!attribute refund_amount_calculator
26:     # Configurable calculator for determining the amount ro refund when
27:     # refunding.
28:     # @return [Class]
29:     class_attribute :refund_amount_calculator
30:     self.refund_amount_calculator = Calculator::Returns::DefaultRefundAmount
31: 
32:     belongs_to :return_authorization, inverse_of: :return_items, optional: true
33:     belongs_to :inventory_unit, inverse_of: :return_items
34:     belongs_to :exchange_variant, class_name: "Spree::Variant", optional: true
35:     belongs_to :exchange_inventory_unit, class_name: "Spree::InventoryUnit", inverse_of: :original_return_item, optional: true
36:     belongs_to :customer_return, inverse_of: :return_items, optional: true
37:     belongs_to :reimbursement, inverse_of: :return_items, optional: true
38:     belongs_to :preferred_reimbursement_type, class_name: "Spree::ReimbursementType", optional: true
39:     belongs_to :override_reimbursement_type, class_name: "Spree::ReimbursementType", optional: true
40:     belongs_to :return_reason, class_name: "Spree::ReturnReason", foreign_key: :return_reason_id, optional: true
41: 
42:     validate :eligible_exchange_variant
43:     validate :belongs_to_same_customer_order
44:     validate :validate_acceptance_status_for_reimbursement
45:     validate :validate_no_other_completed_return_items
46: 
47:     after_create :cancel_others, unless: :cancelled?
48: 
49:     scope :awaiting_return, -> { where(reception_status: "awaiting") }
50:     scope :expecting_return, -> { where.not(reception_status: COMPLETED_RECEPTION_STATUSES) }
51:     scope :not_cancelled, -> { where.not(reception_status: "cancelled") }
52:     scope :valid, -> { where.not(reception_status: %w[cancelled expired unexchanged]) }
53:     scope :not_expired, -> { where.not(reception_status: "expired") }
54:     scope :received, -> { where(reception_status: "received") }
55:     INTERMEDIATE_RECEPTION_STATUSES.each do |reception_status|
56:       scope reception_status, -> { where(reception_status:) }
57:     end
58:     scope :pending, -> { where(acceptance_status: "pending") }
59:     scope :accepted, -> { where(acceptance_status: "accepted") }
60:     scope :rejected, -> { where(acceptance_status: "rejected") }
61:     scope :manual_intervention_required, -> { where(acceptance_status: "manual_intervention_required") }
62:     scope :undecided, -> { where(acceptance_status: %w[pending manual_intervention_required]) }
63:     scope :decided, -> { where.not(acceptance_status: %w[pending manual_intervention_required]) }
64:     scope :reimbursed, -> { where.not(reimbursement_id: nil) }
65:     scope :not_reimbursed, -> { where(reimbursement_id: nil) }
66:     scope :exchange_requested, -> { where.not(exchange_variant: nil) }
67:     scope :exchange_processed, -> { where.not(exchange_inventory_unit: nil) }
68:     scope :exchange_required, -> { exchange_requested.where(exchange_inventory_unit: nil) }
69: 
70:     serialize :acceptance_status_errors, coder: YAML
71: 
72:     delegate :eligible_for_return?, :requires_manual_intervention?, to: :validator
73:     delegate :variant, to: :inventory_unit
74:     delegate :shipment, to: :inventory_unit
75: 
76:     before_create :set_default_amount, unless: :amount_changed?
77:     before_save :set_exchange_amount
78: 
79:     include ::Spree::Config.state_machines.return_item_reception
80:     include ::Spree::Config.state_machines.return_item_acceptance
81: 
82:     extend DisplayMoney
83: 
84:     money_methods :amount, :total, :total_excluding_vat
85: 
86:     # @return [Boolean] true when this retur item is in a complete reception
87:     #   state
88:     def reception_completed?
89:       COMPLETED_RECEPTION_STATUSES.map(&:to_s).include?(reception_status.to_s)
90:     end
91: 
92:     # @param inventory_unit [Spree::InventoryUnit] the inventory for which we
93:     #   want a return item
94:     # @return [Spree::ReturnItem] a valid return item for the given inventory
95:     #   unit if one exists, or a new one if one does not
96:     def self.from_inventory_unit(inventory_unit)
97:       valid.find_by(inventory_unit:) ||
98:         new(inventory_unit:).tap(&:set_default_amount)
99:     end
100: 
101:     # @return [Boolean] true when an exchange has been requested on this return
102:     #   item
103:     def exchange_requested?
104:       exchange_variant.present?
105:     end
106: 
107:     # @return [Boolean] true when an exchange has been processed for this
108:     #   return item
109:     def exchange_processed?
110:       exchange_inventory_unit.present?
111:     end
112: 
113:     # @return [Boolean] true when an exchange has been requested but has yet to
114:     #   be processed
115:     def exchange_required?
116:       exchange_requested? && !exchange_processed?
117:     end
118: 
119:     # @return [BigDecimal] the cost of the item after tax
120:     def total
121:       amount + additional_tax_total
122:     end
123: 
124:     # @return [BigDecimal] the cost of the item before VAT tax
125:     def total_excluding_vat
126:       amount - included_tax_total
127:     end
128: 
129:     # @note This uses the exchange_variant_engine configured on the class.
130:     # @param stock_locations [Array<Spree::StockLocation>] the stock locations to check
131:     # @return [ActiveRecord::Relation<Spree::Variant>] the variants eligible
132:     #   for exchange for this return item
133:     def eligible_exchange_variants(stock_locations = nil)
134:       exchange_variant_engine.eligible_variants(variant, stock_locations:)
135:     end
136: 
137:     # Builds the exchange inventory unit for this return item, only if an
138:     # exchange is required, correctly associating the variant, line item and
139:     # order.
140:     def build_exchange_inventory_unit
141:       # The inventory unit needs to have the new variant
142:       # but it also needs to know the original line item
143:       # for pricing information for if the inventory unit is
144:       # ever returned. This means that the inventory unit's line_item
145:       # will have a different variant than the inventory unit itself
146:       super(variant: exchange_variant, line_item: inventory_unit.line_item) if exchange_required?
147:     end
148: 
149:     # @return [Spree::Shipment, nil] the exchange inventory unit's shipment if it exists
150:     def exchange_shipment
151:       exchange_inventory_unit.try(:shipment)
152:     end
153: 
154:     # Calculates and sets the default amount to be refunded.
155:     #
156:     # @note This uses the configured refund_amount_calculator configured on the
157:     #   class.
158:     def set_default_amount
159:       self.amount = refund_amount_calculator.new.compute(self)
160:     end
161: 
162:     def potential_reception_transitions
163:       status_paths = reception_status_paths.to_states
164:       event_paths = reception_status_paths.events
165:       status_paths.delete(:cancelled)
166:       status_paths.delete(:expired)
167:       status_paths.delete(:unexchanged)
168:       event_paths.delete(:cancel)
169:       event_paths.delete(:expired)
170:       event_paths.delete(:unexchange)
171: 
172:       status_paths.map { |status| I18n.t("spree.reception_states.#{status}", default: status.to_s.humanize) }.zip(event_paths)
173:     end
174: 
175:     def part_of_exchange?
176:       # test whether this ReturnItem was either a) one for which an exchange was sent or
177:       #   b) the exchanged item itself being returned in lieu of the original item
178:       exchange_requested? || sibling_intended_for_exchange("unexchanged")
179:     end
180: 
181:     def currency
182:       return_authorization.try(:currency) || Spree::Config[:currency]
183:     end
184: 
185:     private
186: 
187:     def persist_acceptance_status_errors
188:       update(acceptance_status_errors: validator.errors)
189:     end
190: 
191:     def process_inventory_unit!
192:       inventory_unit.return!
193: 
194:       if should_restock?
195:         customer_return.stock_location.restock(inventory_unit.variant, 1, customer_return)
196:       end
197: 
198:       customer_return&.process_return!
199:     end
200: 
201:     def sibling_intended_for_exchange(status)
202:       # This happens when we ship an exchange to a customer, but the customer keeps the original and returns the exchange
203:       self.class.find_by(reception_status: status, exchange_inventory_unit: inventory_unit)
204:     end
205: 
206:     def check_unexchange
207:       original_ri = sibling_intended_for_exchange("awaiting")
208:       if original_ri
209:         original_ri.unexchange!
210:         set_default_amount
211:         save!
212:       end
213:     end
214: 
215:     # This logic is also present in the customer return. The reason for the
216:     # duplication and not having a validates_associated on the customer_return
217:     # is that it would lead to duplicate error messages for the customer return.
218:     # Not specifying a stock location for example would add an error message about
219:     # the mandatory field when validating the customer return and again when saving
220:     # the associated return items.
221:     def belongs_to_same_customer_order
222:       return unless customer_return && inventory_unit
223: 
224:       if customer_return.order_id != inventory_unit.order_id
225:         errors.add(:base, I18n.t("spree.return_items_cannot_be_associated_with_multiple_orders"))
226:       end
227:     end
228: 
229:     def eligible_exchange_variant
230:       return unless exchange_variant && exchange_variant_id_changed?
231:       unless eligible_exchange_variants.include?(exchange_variant)
232:         errors.add(:base, I18n.t("spree.invalid_exchange_variant"))
233:       end
234:     end
235: 
236:     def validator
237:       @validator ||= return_eligibility_validator.new(self)
238:     end
239: 
240:     def validate_acceptance_status_for_reimbursement
241:       if reimbursement && !accepted?
242:         errors.add(:reimbursement, :cannot_be_associated_unless_accepted)
243:       end
244:     end
245: 
246:     def set_exchange_amount
247:       self.amount = BigDecimal("0.0") if exchange_requested?
248:     end
249: 
250:     def validate_no_other_completed_return_items
251:       other_return_item = Spree::ReturnItem.where({
252:         inventory_unit_id:,
253:         reception_status: COMPLETED_RECEPTION_STATUSES
254:       }).where.not(id:).first
255: 
256:       if other_return_item && (new_record? || COMPLETED_RECEPTION_STATUSES.include?(reception_status.to_sym))
257:         errors.add(:inventory_unit, :other_completed_return_item_exists,
258:           inventory_unit_id:,
259:           return_item_id: other_return_item.id)
260:       end
261:     end
262: 
263:     def cancel_others
264:       Spree::ReturnItem.where(inventory_unit_id:)
265:         .where.not(id:)
266:         .valid
267:         .each(&:cancel!)
268:     end
269: 
270:     def should_restock?
271:       customer_return &&
272:         resellable? &&
273:         variant.should_track_inventory? &&
274:         customer_return.stock_location.restock_inventory?
275:     end
276:   end
277: end

(End of file - total 277 lines)
</content>

<path>/Users/luc/Developer/luuuc/oss/sense-benchmark/baseline/solidus/core/app/models/spree/wallet/add_payment_sources_to_wallet.rb</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: # This class is responsible for saving payment sources in the user's "wallet"
4: # for future use.  You can substitute your own class via
5: # `Spree::Config.add_payment_sources_to_wallet_class`.
6: class Spree::Wallet::AddPaymentSourcesToWallet
7:   def initialize(order)
8:     @order = order
9:   end
10: 
11:   # This is called after an order transistions to complete and should save the
12:   # order's payment source in the user's "wallet" for future use.
13:   #
14:   # @return [void]
15:   def add_to_wallet
16:     if !order.temporary_payment_source && order.user
17:       # select valid sources
18:       payments = order.payments.valid
19:       sources = payments.map(&:source)
20:         .uniq
21:         .compact
22:         .select { |payment| payment.try(:reusable?) }
23: 
24:       # add valid sources to wallet and optionally set a default
25:       if sources.any?
26:         # arbitrarily sort by id for picking a default
27:         order_wallet_payment_sources = sources.sort_by(&:id).map do |source|
28:           order.user.wallet.add(source)
29:         end
30: 
31:         make_default(order_wallet_payment_sources)
32:       end
33:     end
34:   end
35: 
36:   protected
37: 
38:   def make_default(sources)
39:     order.user.wallet.default_wallet_payment_source = sources.last
40:   end
41: 
42:   private
43: 
44:   attr_reader :order
45: end

(End of file - total 45 lines)
</content>

<path>/Users/luc/Developer/luuuc/oss/sense-benchmark/baseline/solidus/core/app/models/spree/in_memory_order_updater.rb</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require "spree/manipulative_query_monitor"
4: 
5: module Spree
6:   class InMemoryOrderUpdater
7:     attr_reader :order
8: 
9:     # logs a warning when a manipulative query is made when the persist flag is set to false
10:     class_attribute :log_manipulative_queries
11:     self.log_manipulative_queries = true
12: 
13:     delegate :payments, :line_items, :adjustments, :all_adjustments, :shipments, :quantity, to: :order
14: 
15:     def initialize(order)
16:       @order = order
17:     end
18: 
19:     # This is a multi-purpose method for processing logic related to changes in the Order.
20:     # It is meant to be called from various observers so that the Order is aware of changes
21:     # that affect totals and other values stored in the Order.
22:     #
23:     # This method should never do anything to the Order that results in a save call on the
24:     # object with callbacks (otherwise you will end up in an infinite recursion as the
25:     # associations try to save and then in turn try to call +update!+ again.)
26:     def recalculate(persist: true)
27:       monitor =
28:         if log_manipulative_queries
29:           Spree::ManipulativeQueryMonitor
30:         else
31:           proc { |&block| block.call }
32:         end
33: 
34:       order.transaction do
35:         monitor.call do
36:           recalculate_line_item_prices
37:           recalculate_item_count
38:           assign_shipment_amounts
39:         end
40: 
41:         if persist
42:           update_totals(persist:)
43:         else
44:           monitor.call do
45:             update_totals(persist:)
46:           end
47:         end
48: 
49:         monitor.call do
50:           if order.completed?
51:             recalculate_payment_state
52:             recalculate_shipment_state
53:           end
54:         end
55: 
56:         persist_totals if persist
57:       end
58: 
59:       Spree::Bus.publish(:order_recalculated, order:) if persist
60:     end
61:     alias_method :update, :recalculate
62:     deprecate update: :recalculate, deprecator: Spree.deprecator
63: 
64:     # Recalculates the state on all of them shipments, then recalculates the
65:     # +shipment_state+ attribute according to the following logic:
66:     #
67:     # shipped   when all Shipments are in the "shipped" state
68:     # partial   when at least one Shipment has a state of "shipped" and there is another Shipment with a state other than "shipped"
69:     #           or there are InventoryUnits associated with the order that have a state of "sold" but are not associated with a Shipment.
70:     # ready     when all Shipments are in the "ready" state
71:     # backorder when there is backordered inventory associated with an order
72:     # pending   when all Shipments are in the "pending" state
73:     #
74:     # The +shipment_state+ value helps with reporting, etc. since it provides a quick and easy way to locate Orders needing attention.
75:     def recalculate_shipment_state
76:       shipments.each(&:recalculate_state)
77: 
78:       order.shipment_state = determine_shipment_state
79:       order.shipment_state
80:     end
81:     alias_method :update_shipment_state, :recalculate_shipment_state
82:     deprecate update_shipment_state: :recalculate_shipment_state, deprecator: Spree.deprecator
83: 
84:     # Recalculates the +payment_state+ attribute according to the following logic:
85:     #
86:     # paid          when +payment_total+ is equal to +total+
87:     # balance_due   when +payment_total+ is less than +total+
88:     # credit_owed   when +payment_total+ is greater than +total+
89:     # failed        when most recent payment is in the failed state
90:     # void          when the order has been canceled and the payment total is 0
91:     #
92:     # The +payment_state+ value helps with reporting, etc. since it provides a quick and easy way to locate Orders needing attention.
93:     def recalculate_payment_state
94:       order.payment_state = determine_payment_state
95:       order.payment_state
96:     end
97:     alias_method :update_payment_state, :recalculate_payment_state
98:     deprecate update_payment_state: :recalculate_payment_state, deprecator: Spree.deprecator
99: 
100:     private
101: 
102:     def determine_payment_state
103:       if payments.present? && payments.valid.empty? && order.outstanding_balance != 0
104:         "failed"
105:       elsif order.state == "canceled" && order.payment_total.zero?
106:         "void"
107:       elsif order.outstanding_balance > 0
108:         "balance_due"
109:       elsif order.outstanding_balance < 0
110:         "credit_owed"
111:       else
112:         # outstanding_balance == 0
113:         "paid"
114:       end
115:     end
116: 
117:     def determine_shipment_state
118:       if order.backordered?
119:         "backorder"
120:       else
121:         # get all the shipment states for this order
122:         shipment_states = shipments.states
123:         if shipment_states.size > 1
124:           # multiple shiment states means it's most likely partially shipped
125:           "partial"
126:         else
127:           # will return nil if no shipments are found
128:           shipment_states.first
129:         end
130:       end
131:     end
132: 
133:     # This will update and select the best promotion adjustment, update tax
134:     # adjustments, update cancellation adjustments, and then update the total
135:     # fields (promo_total, included_tax_total, additional_tax_total, and
136:     # adjustment_total) on the item.
137:     # @return [void]
138:     def update_adjustments(persist:)
139:       # Promotion adjustments must be applied first, then tax adjustments.
140:       # This fits the criteria for VAT tax as outlined here:
141:       # http://www.hmrc.gov.uk/vat/managing/charging/discounts-etc.htm#1
142:       # It also fits the criteria for sales tax as outlined here:
143:       # http://www.boe.ca.gov/formspubs/pub113/
144:       update_promotions(persist:)
145:       update_tax_adjustments
146:       assign_item_totals
147:     end
148: 
149:     # Updates the following Order total values:
150:     #
151:     # +payment_total+      The total value of all finalized Payments (NOTE: non-finalized Payments are excluded)
152:     # +item_total+         The total value of all LineItems
153:     # +adjustment_total+   The total value of all adjustments (promotions, credits, etc.)
154:     # +promo_total+        The total value of all promotion adjustments
155:     # +total+              The so-called "order total."  This is equivalent to +item_total+ plus +adjustment_total+.
156:     def update_totals(persist:)
157:       recalculate_payment_total
158:       recalculate_item_total
159:       recalculate_shipment_total
160:       update_adjustment_total(persist:)
161:     end
162: 
163:     def assign_shipment_amounts
164:       shipments.each(&:assign_amounts)
165:     end
166: 
167:     def update_adjustment_total(persist:)
168:       update_adjustments(persist:)
169: 
170:       all_items = line_items + shipments
171:       # Ignore any adjustments that have been marked for destruction in our
172:       # calculations. They'll get removed when/if we persist the order.
173:       valid_adjustments = adjustments.reject(&:marked_for_destruction?)
174:       order_tax_adjustments = valid_adjustments.select(&:tax?)
175: 
176:       order.adjustment_total = all_items.sum(&:adjustment_total) + valid_adjustments.sum(&:amount)
177:       order.included_tax_total = all_items.sum(&:included_tax_total) + order_tax_adjustments.select(&:included?).sum(&:amount)
178:       order.additional_tax_total = all_items.sum(&:additional_tax_total) + order_tax_adjustments.reject(&:included?).sum(&:amount)
179: 
180:       recalculate_order_total
181:     end
182: 
183:     def update_promotions(persist:)
184:       Spree::Config.promotions.order_adjuster_class.new(order).call(persist:)
185:     end
186: 
187:     def update_tax_adjustments
188:       Spree::Config.tax_adjuster_class.new(order).adjust!
189:     end
190: 
191:     def update_cancellations
192:     end
193:     deprecate :update_cancellations, deprecator: Spree.deprecator
194: 
195:     def assign_item_totals
196:       [*line_items, *shipments].each do |item|
197:         Spree::Config.item_total_class.new(item).recalculate!
198: 
199:         next unless item.changed?
200: 
201:         item.assign_attributes(
202:           promo_total: item.promo_total,
203:           included_tax_total: item.included_tax_total,
204:           additional_tax_total: item.additional_tax_total,
205:           adjustment_total: item.adjustment_total
206:         )
207:       end
208:     end
209: 
210:     def recalculate_payment_total
211:       order.payment_total = payments.completed.includes(:refunds).sum { |payment| payment.amount - payment.refunds.sum(:amount) }
212:     end
213: 
214:     def recalculate_shipment_total
215:       order.shipment_total = shipments.to_a.sum(&:cost)
216:       recalculate_order_total
217:     end
218: 
219:     def recalculate_order_total
220:       order.total = order.item_total + order.shipment_total + order.adjustment_total
221:     end
222: 
223:     def recalculate_item_count
224:       order.item_count = line_items.to_a.sum(&:quantity)
225:     end
226: 
227:     def recalculate_item_total
228:       order.item_total = line_items.to_a.sum(&:amount)
229:       recalculate_order_total
230:     end
231: 
232:     def recalculate_line_item_prices
233:       if Spree::Config.recalculate_cart_prices
234:         line_items.each(&:recalculate_price)
235:       end
236:     end
237: 
238:     def persist_totals
239:       log_state_change("payment")
240:       log_state_change("shipment")
241:       order.save!
242:     end
243: 
244:     def log_state_change(name)
245:       state = "#{name}_state"
246:       previous_state, current_state = order.changes[state]
247: 
248:       if previous_state != current_state
249:         # Enqueue the job to track this state change
250:         StateChangeTrackingJob.perform_later(
251:           order,
252:           previous_state,
253:           current_state,
254:           Time.current,
255:           name
256:         )
257:       end
258:     end
259:   end
260: end

(End of file - total 260 lines)
</content>

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   # Finds orders to merge when a user logs in.
5:   #
6:   # Configurable via {Spree::Config#mergeable_orders_finder_class}.
7:   # Default behavior finds all incomplete orders from the same store.
8:   #
9:   # @example Custom finder for recent orders only
10:   #   class RecentOrdersFinder
11:   #     def initialize(context:)
12:   #       @user = context.spree_current_user
13:   #       @store = context.current_store
14:   #       @current_order = context.current_order
15:   #     end
16:   #
17:   #     def call
18:   #       @user.orders.by_store(@store).incomplete
19:   #            .where.not(id: @current_order.id)
20:   #            .where('created_at > ?', 7.days.ago)
21:   #     end
22:   #   end
23:   #
24:   #   Spree::Config.mergeable_orders_finder_class = RecentOrdersFinder
25:   class MergeableOrdersFinder
26:     # @param context [Object] an object that responds to spree_current_user,
27:     #   current_store, and current_order (typically a controller)
28:     def initialize(context:)
29:       @user = context.spree_current_user
30:       @store = context.current_store
31:       @current_order = context.current_order
32:     end
33: 
34:     # Returns orders that should be merged into the current order
35:     #
36:     # @return [ActiveRecord::Relation<Spree::Order>] incomplete orders from the
37:     # same store
38:     def call
39:       return Spree::Order.none unless @user && @current_order
40: 
41:       @user.orders.by_store(@store).incomplete.where(frontend_viewable: true).where.not(id: @current_order.id)
42:     end
43:   end
44: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   module StateChangeTracking
5:     extend ActiveSupport::Concern
6: 
7:     included do
8:       after_update :enqueue_state_change_tracking, if: :track_state_change?
9:     end
10: 
11:     private
12: 
13:     def track_state_change?
14:       saved_change_to_state?
15:     end
16: 
17:     # Enqueue background job to track state changes asynchronously
18:     def enqueue_state_change_tracking
19:       previous_state, current_state = saved_changes["state"]
20: 
21:       # Enqueue the job to track this state change
22:       Spree::Config.state_change_tracking_class.call(
23:         stateful: self,
24:         previous_state:,
25:         current_state:,
26:         transition_timestamp: Time.current
27:       )
28:     end
29:   end
30: end

370:     # Allows providing your own Mailer for order mailer.
371:     #
372:     # @!attribute [rw] order_mailer_class
373:     # @return [ActionMailer::Base] an object that responds to "confirm_email",
374:     #   "cancel_email" and "inventory_cancellation_email"
375:     #   (e.g. an ActionMailer with a "confirm_email" method) with the same
376:     #   signature as Spree::OrderMailer.confirm_email.
377:     class_name_attribute :order_mailer_class, default: "Spree::OrderMailer"
378: 
379:     # Allows providing your own order update attributes class for checkout.
380:     #
381:     # @!attribute [rw] order_update_attributes_class
382:     # @return [Class] a class that responds to "call"
383:     #   with the same signature as Spree::OrderUpdateAttributes.
384:     class_name_attribute :order_update_attributes_class, default: "Spree::OrderUpdateAttributes"
385: 
386:     # Allows providing a different order recalculator.
387:     # @!attribute [rw] order_recalculator_class
388:     # @see Spree::OrderUpdater
389:     # @return [Class] an object that conforms to the API of
390:     #   the standard order recalculator class
391:     #   Spree::OrderUpdater.
392:     class_name_attribute :order_recalculator_class, default: "Spree::OrderUpdater"
393: 
394:     # Allows providing your own Mailer for reimbursement mailer.
395:     #
396:     # @!attribute [rw] reimbursement_mailer_class
397:     # @return [ActionMailer::Base] an object that responds to "reimbursement_email"
398:     #   (e.g. an ActionMailer with a "reimbursement_email" method) with the same
399:     #   signature as Spree::ReimbursementMailer.reimbursement_email.
400:     class_name_attribute :reimbursement_mailer_class, default: "Spree::ReimbursementMailer"
401: 
402:     # Allows providing your own Mailer for shipped cartons.
403:     #
404:     # @!attribute [rw] carton_shipped_email_class
405:     # @return [ActionMailer::Base] an object that responds to "shipped_email"
406:     #   (e.g. an ActionMailer with a "shipped_email" method) with the same
407:     #   signature as Spree::CartonMailer.shipped_email.
408:     class_name_attribute :carton_shipped_email_class, default: "Spree::CartonMailer"
409: 
410:     # Allows providing your own class for merging two orders.
